1.text: After I, Nephi, had finished teaching my brothers, our father, Lehi, also spoke many things to them. He told them about all the great things the Lord had done for them in bringing them out of the land of Jerusalem.
He spoke to them about their rebellions on the waters and God's mercy in sparing their lives so they were not swallowed up in the sea.
He also spoke to them about the promised land they had obtained — how merciful the Lord had been in warning us to flee from Jerusalem.
"For I have seen a vision," he said, "in which I know that Jerusalem is destroyed. If we had stayed in Jerusalem, we would have died too."

"But despite our hardships," he said, "we have obtained a promised land — a land that is better than all other lands. The Lord God has promised this land to me as an inheritance for my descendants. He has promised this land to me, to my children forever, and to all those who would be led out of other countries by the Lord's hand."
"Therefore, I, Lehi, prophesy by the Spirit within me that no one will come to this land unless they are brought by the hand of the Lord."

"This land is set apart for whoever the Lord brings here. If they serve him according to the commandments he has given, it will be a land of freedom for them. They will never be brought into captivity — unless it is because of their own wickedness. If wickedness increases, the land will be cursed for their sakes, but for the righteous it will be blessed forever."
"It is wise that this land be kept hidden from the knowledge of other nations for now, because many nations would overrun the land, leaving no room for an inheritance."

"I, Lehi, have obtained a promise that as long as those the Lord brings out of Jerusalem keep his commandments, they will prosper in this land. They will be protected from all other nations so they can possess this land for themselves. If they keep his commandments, they will be blessed in this land and will live in peace forever, with no one to bother them or take away their inheritance."

"But when the time comes that they dwindle in unbelief — after receiving such great blessings from the Lord, having knowledge of the creation of the earth and all people, knowing God's great and marvelous works, having power to do all things by faith, having all the commandments from the beginning, and being brought by his infinite goodness into this precious promised land — I say, if the day comes that they reject the Holy One of Israel, the true Messiah, their Redeemer and their God, then the judgments of a just God will rest upon them."
"He will bring other nations against them and give those nations power. He will take away their lands and cause them to be scattered and struck down."
"As one generation passes to another, there will be bloodshed and great suffering among them. Therefore, my sons, I want you to remember. I want you to listen to my words."

"I wish you would wake up — wake from a deep sleep, even from the sleep of hell! Shake off the terrible chains that bind you, the chains that bind the children of men and carry them away captive down to the eternal gulf of misery and sorrow."

"Wake up! Rise from the dust and hear the words of your trembling parent, whose body you will soon lay down in the cold and silent grave, from which no traveler returns. In a few more days, I will go the way of all the earth."
"But the Lord has redeemed my soul from hell. I have seen his glory, and I am forever encircled in the arms of his love."
"I want you to remember to keep the Lord's statutes and judgments. This has been my deepest concern from the very beginning."

"My heart has been weighed down with sorrow time and again, because I have feared that due to the hardness of your hearts, the Lord your God would come upon you in the fullness of his wrath, and you would be cut off and destroyed forever."
"Or that a curse would come upon you for many generations, and you would be afflicted by war, famine, hatred, and be led according to the will and captivity of the devil."
"My sons, I hope these things do not come upon you, but that you might be a chosen and favored people of the Lord. But his will be done, for his ways are righteous forever."
"He has said: 'As long as you keep my commandments, you will prosper in the land. But if you will not keep my commandments, you will be cut off from my presence.'"

"So that my soul might have joy in you, and that my heart might leave this world with gladness because of you — so I will not be brought down with grief and sorrow to the grave — rise from the dust, my sons! Be men! Be determined, united in one mind and one heart in all things, so that you will not fall into captivity."

"So that you will not be cursed with a severe curse, and so that you will not bring the displeasure of a just God upon you, leading to the destruction — the eternal destruction — of both soul and body."
"Wake up, my sons! Put on the armor of righteousness. Shake off the chains that bind you and come out of obscurity. Rise from the dust!"

"Stop rebelling against your brother, whose visions have been glorious and who has kept the commandments since we left Jerusalem. He has been an instrument in God's hands in bringing us to this promised land. Without him, we would have died of hunger in the wilderness. Yet you tried to take his life, and he has suffered much sorrow because of you."

"I am deeply afraid and tremble because of you, worried that Nephi will suffer again. You have accused him of seeking power and authority over you, but I know he has not sought power or authority over you. He has sought only the glory of God and your eternal well-being."

"You have complained because he has been direct with you. You say he has been harsh and angry with you. But his sharpness was the power of God's word within him, and what you call anger was the truth according to God, which he could not hold back. He was boldly showing you your sins."

"The power of God had to be with him, even to the point of commanding you to obey. But it was not him — it was the Spirit of the Lord within him that opened his mouth to speak, and he could not hold it back."

"Now my son Laman, and also Lemuel and Sam, and also my sons who are the sons of Ishmael — if you will listen to the voice of Nephi, you will not be destroyed. If you will listen to him, I leave you a blessing — even my firstborn blessing."
"But if you will not listen to him, I take away my firstborn blessing, and it will rest upon him instead."
"And now, Zoram, I speak to you. You were the servant of Laban, but you have been brought out of the land of Jerusalem, and I know you are a true friend to my son Nephi, forever."

"Because you have been faithful, your descendants will be blessed along with Nephi's descendants. They will live in prosperity for a long time in this land, and nothing except their own wickedness will harm or disturb their prosperity in this land forever."
"If you keep the Lord's commandments, the Lord has set apart this land as a safe home for your descendants and the descendants of my son."

2.text: Now, Jacob, I speak to you. You are my firstborn in the days of my hardship in the wilderness. In your childhood, you suffered afflictions and much sorrow because of the cruelty of your brothers.
Nevertheless, Jacob, my firstborn in the wilderness, you know the greatness of God, and he will turn your afflictions to your benefit.

Your soul will be blessed, and you will live safely with your brother Nephi. Your days will be spent in the service of your God. I know you are redeemed because of the righteousness of your Redeemer, for you have seen that in the fullness of time he comes to bring salvation to mankind.

You have seen his glory in your youth, so you are blessed just as those to whom he will minister in the flesh. For the Spirit is the same yesterday, today, and forever. The way has been prepared since the fall of man, and salvation is free.

People are taught enough to know good from evil. The law is given to mankind. But by the law alone, no one is justified. By the law, people are cut off. By the temporal law they were cut off, and by the spiritual law they fall away from what is good and become miserable forever.
Therefore, redemption comes through the Holy Messiah, for he is full of grace and truth.
He offers himself as a sacrifice for sin, to satisfy the demands of the law, for all those who have a broken heart and a contrite spirit. For no one else can the demands of the law be satisfied.

How incredibly important it is to make these things known to the people of the earth! They need to know that no person can live in God's presence except through the merits, mercy, and grace of the Holy Messiah, who lays down his life according to the flesh and takes it up again by the power of the Spirit, so he can bring about the resurrection of the dead, being the first to rise.
He is the firstfruits unto God, since he will intercede for all the children of men. Those who believe in him will be saved.

Because of his intercession for all, everyone comes before God to be judged according to the truth and holiness in him. The demands of the law that the Holy One has given — the punishment that is assigned, which stands opposite to the happiness that is assigned — are fulfilled through the Atonement.

There must be opposition in all things. If not, my firstborn in the wilderness, there could be no righteousness or wickedness, no holiness or misery, no good or bad. Everything would be blended into one. If it were all one thing, it would be as if dead — having no life or death, no corruption or purity, no happiness or misery, no feeling at all.

If that were the case, creation would have been for nothing. There would have been no purpose in it. This idea would destroy the wisdom of God and his eternal purposes, and also his power, mercy, and justice.

If you say there is no law, you must also say there is no sin. If you say there is no sin, you must also say there is no righteousness. If there is no righteousness, there is no happiness. If there is no righteousness or happiness, there is no punishment or misery. If these things do not exist, there is no God. And if there is no God, we do not exist, neither does the earth. Nothing could have been created — nothing to act and nothing to be acted upon. Everything would have vanished away.

Now, my sons, I am telling you these things for your benefit and learning. There is a God, and he has created all things — the heavens, the earth, and everything in them — both things that act and things that are acted upon.

To bring about his eternal purposes for mankind, after he had created our first parents, the animals of the field, the birds of the air, and everything that is created, there had to be opposition. The forbidden fruit stood in opposition to the tree of life — one being sweet and the other bitter.
The Lord God gave mankind the ability to act for themselves. But people could not act for themselves unless they were drawn by one thing or the other.
I, Lehi, based on what I have read, must conclude that an angel of God, according to the scriptures, fell from heaven and became a devil, having chosen evil before God.

Because he had fallen from heaven and become miserable forever, he wanted all mankind to be miserable too. So he — that old serpent, the devil, the father of all lies — said to Eve: "Eat the forbidden fruit and you will not die, but you will be like God, knowing good and evil."
After Adam and Eve ate the forbidden fruit, they were driven out of the Garden of Eden to work the earth.
They had children — the entire human family.

The lives of mankind were extended according to God's will so they could repent while in the flesh. Their lives became a time of testing, and their time was lengthened according to the commandments God gave to mankind. He commanded that everyone must repent, for he showed all people that they were lost because of their first parents' transgression.

If Adam had not transgressed, he would not have fallen. He would have remained in the Garden of Eden. Everything that was created would have remained in the same state it was in after it was created — it would have stayed that way forever with no end.
They would have had no children. They would have remained in a state of innocence, having no joy because they knew no misery, doing no good because they knew no sin.
But all things have been done in the wisdom of him who knows all things.
Adam fell so that mankind could exist, and mankind exists so that they might have joy.

The Messiah comes in the fullness of time to redeem the children of men from the fall. Because they are redeemed from the fall, they have become free forever, knowing good from evil — free to act for themselves and not be acted upon, except by the punishment of the law at the great and last day, according to God's commandments.

Therefore, people are free in the flesh. All things that are good for them are given to them. They are free to choose liberty and eternal life through the great Mediator of all men, or to choose captivity and death according to the captivity and power of the devil, who wants all people to be miserable like himself.
Now, my sons, I want you to look to the great Mediator and listen to his great commandments. Be faithful to his words and choose eternal life, according to the will of his Holy Spirit.

And do not choose eternal death, according to the will of the flesh and the evil in it, which gives the spirit of the devil power to captivate you and bring you down to hell, where he may rule over you in his own kingdom.

I have spoken these few words to all of you, my sons, in the last days of my life. I have chosen the good part, according to the words of the prophet. My only desire is the everlasting welfare of your souls. Amen.

3.text: Now I speak to you, Joseph, my last-born. You were born in the wilderness during my afflictions — in the days of my greatest sorrow, your mother bore you.

May the Lord also set apart this land for you — this most precious land — as an inheritance for you, your descendants, and your brothers, for your safety forever, if you keep the commandments of the Holy One of Israel.
Now, Joseph, my last-born, whom I have brought out of the wilderness of my afflictions, may the Lord bless you forever, for your descendants will not be completely destroyed.
You are my child, and I am a descendant of Joseph who was taken captive into Egypt. Great were the covenants the Lord made with that Joseph.

Joseph of old truly saw our day. He obtained a promise from the Lord that from his descendants, the Lord God would raise up a righteous branch to the house of Israel — not the Messiah, but a branch that would be broken off yet remembered in the Lord's covenants. The Messiah would be made known to them in the latter days, in the spirit of power, bringing them from darkness into light — from hidden darkness and from captivity into freedom.
Joseph truly testified, saying: "The Lord my God will raise up a seer — a special seer for my descendants."

Joseph truly said: "The Lord said to me: 'I will raise up a special seer from your descendants. He will be highly valued among your descendants. I will give him a commandment to do a work for your descendants — his brethren — that will be of great worth to them, even bringing them to a knowledge of the covenants I have made with your fathers.'"
"I will give him a commandment that he will do no other work except the work I command him. I will make him great in my eyes, for he will do my work."
"He will be great like Moses, whom I have said I would raise up to deliver my people, O house of Israel."
"I will raise up Moses to deliver your people out of the land of Egypt."

"But I will also raise up a seer from your descendants. I will give him power to bring forth my word to your descendants — not only to bring forth my word," says the Lord, "but to convince them of my word that has already gone forth among them."

"Your descendants will write, and the descendants of Judah will write. What is written by your descendants, together with what is written by the descendants of Judah, will come together to disprove false doctrines, end arguments, establish peace among your descendants, and bring them to a knowledge of their fathers in the latter days — and also to a knowledge of my covenants," says the Lord.
"Out of weakness he will be made strong, on the day when my work begins among all my people, to restore you, O house of Israel," says the Lord.

Joseph prophesied, saying: "The Lord will bless that seer, and those who try to destroy him will be defeated. For this promise, which I have obtained from the Lord concerning my descendants, will be fulfilled. I am certain this promise will be fulfilled."

"His name will be the same as mine, and it will also be the name of his father. He will be like me, for the thing the Lord brings forth by his hand, by the Lord's power, will bring my people to salvation."
Joseph prophesied: "I am sure of this, just as I am sure of the promise of Moses. The Lord has said to me: 'I will preserve your descendants forever.'"

"The Lord has said: 'I will raise up a Moses. I will give him power through a rod, and I will give him the ability to write judgments. But I will not make him a powerful speaker — I will not make him mighty in speaking. Instead, I will write my law for him with my own finger, and I will provide a spokesman for him.'"

"The Lord also said to me: 'I will raise up someone from your descendants, and I will provide a spokesman for him. I will give him the ability to write the words of your descendants for your descendants, and the spokesman from your descendants will declare it.'"
"The words he writes will be the words that, in my wisdom, should go to your descendants. It will be as if your descendants had cried out to them from the dust, for I know their faith."
"They will cry from the dust — a cry of repentance to their brethren, even after many generations have passed. And their cry will go forth, even in the simpleness of their words."

"Because of their faith, their words will come from my mouth to their brethren who are your descendants. I will make their weak words strong through their faith, leading to the remembering of my covenant that I made with your fathers."
"Now, my son Joseph, this is how my ancient father prophesied."
"Because of this covenant, you are blessed. Your descendants will not be destroyed, for they will listen to the words of the book."

"A mighty one will arise among them who will do much good, both in word and in deed. He will be an instrument in God's hands, with extraordinary faith, to work mighty wonders and accomplish great things in God's sight, bringing about much restoration to the house of Israel and to your brothers' descendants."

"Now, you are blessed, Joseph. You are still young, so listen to the words of your brother Nephi, and it will be done for you according to the words I have spoken. Remember the words of your dying father. Amen."

4.text: Now I, Nephi, want to talk about the prophecies my father spoke about concerning Joseph, who was taken into Egypt.

Joseph truly prophesied about all of his descendants. The prophecies he wrote are among the greatest ever recorded. He prophesied about us and our future generations, and these prophecies are written on the plates of brass.

So after my father finished speaking about Joseph's prophecies, he called the children of Laman—his sons and daughters—and said to them: Listen, my sons and daughters, who are the children of my firstborn. I want you to pay attention to my words.
For the Lord God has said: As long as you keep my commandments, you will prosper in the land; but if you do not keep my commandments, you will be cut off from my presence.
But listen, my sons and daughters, I cannot die without leaving a blessing for you; for I know that if you are raised in the right way, you will not turn away from it.
Therefore, even if you are cursed, I leave my blessing upon you so that the curse may be lifted from you and fall instead upon your parents.
Because of my blessing, the Lord God will not allow you to be destroyed; He will be merciful to you and to your descendants forever.
After my father finished speaking to the sons and daughters of Laman, he had the sons and daughters of Lemuel brought before him.

He spoke to them, saying: Listen, my sons and daughters, who are the children of my second son. I give you the same blessing I gave to the children of Laman. You will not be completely destroyed, and in the end your descendants will be blessed.
When my father finished speaking to them, he spoke to the sons of Ishmael and to all of Ishmael's household.

After he finished speaking to them, he spoke to Sam, saying: You are blessed, and so are your descendants, for you will inherit the land just like your brother Nephi. Your descendants will be counted with his descendants, and you will be like your brother, and your family like his family, and you will be blessed all your days.
After my father Lehi had spoken to all his household, according to the feelings of his heart and the Spirit of the Lord that was in him, he grew old. And he died and was buried.
Not many days after his death, Laman and Lemuel and the sons of Ishmael became angry with me because of the Lord's warnings.

For I, Nephi, felt compelled to speak to them according to God's word. I had told them many things, and so had my father before his death. Many of those things are written on my other plates, for the more historical account is recorded there.

On these plates I write the things closest to my heart, along with many scriptures engraved on the plates of brass. For my soul delights in the scriptures, and my heart thinks deeply about them, and I write them down for the learning and benefit of my children.
My soul delights in the things of the Lord, and my heart continually thinks about the things I have seen and heard.

Yet despite the great goodness of the Lord in showing me His great and marvelous works, my heart cries out: What a miserable person I am! My heart is sorrowful because of my weaknesses; my soul grieves because of my sins.
I am surrounded by temptations and sins that so easily overtake me.
When I want to rejoice, my heart groans because of my sins. Nevertheless, I know in whom I have put my trust.
My God has been my support. He has led me through my hardships in the wilderness, and He has kept me safe upon the waters of the great ocean.
He has filled me with His love, so powerfully that it overwhelmed my whole being.
He has defeated my enemies and caused them to tremble before me.
He has heard my cries during the day, and He has given me knowledge through visions in the night.
During the day I have prayed boldly and powerfully before Him. I have sent my voice up to heaven, and angels came down and served me.
By the power of His Spirit, my body has been carried away to extremely high mountains. My eyes have seen great things—things too great for any person to describe—so I was told not to write them down.

If I have seen such great things, and if the Lord in His kindness toward His children has visited people with so much mercy, then why should my heart weep and my soul stay in the valley of sorrow? Why should my body waste away and my strength fail because of my troubles?

Why should I give in to sin because of my weakness? Why should I surrender to temptation and let the evil one take over my heart, destroying my peace and troubling my soul? Why do I let myself get angry because of my enemy?
Wake up, my soul! Stop sinking into sin. Rejoice, my heart, and give no more room to the enemy of my soul.
Do not become angry again because of my enemies. Do not let my strength weaken because of my troubles.
Rejoice, my heart, and cry out to the Lord, saying: O Lord, I will praise you forever! Yes, my soul will rejoice in you, my God, the rock of my salvation.
O Lord, will you save my soul? Will you rescue me from my enemies? Will you help me to tremble at even the appearance of sin?

May the gates of hell be shut continually before me, because my heart is broken and my spirit is humble! O Lord, please do not shut the gates of your righteousness before me, so that I may walk in the lowly valley and stay on the straight path!

O Lord, will you wrap me in the robe of your righteousness! O Lord, will you make a way for me to escape from my enemies! Will you make my path straight before me! Do not place a stumbling block in my way—but clear my way before me, and block not my way, but the ways of my enemy.

O Lord, I have trusted in you, and I will trust in you forever. I will not put my trust in human strength, for I know that cursed is the person who puts their trust in human strength. Yes, cursed is the one who puts their trust in people or makes human power their support.

Yes, I know that God gives generously to those who ask. My God will give to me if I ask for the right things. Therefore I will lift up my voice to you; I will cry out to you, my God, the rock of my righteousness. My voice will rise up to you forever, my rock and my everlasting God. Amen.

5.text: It happened that I, Nephi, prayed earnestly to the Lord my God because of the anger of my brothers.
But their anger only increased against me, so much that they tried to take my life.

They complained against me, saying: Our younger brother thinks he can rule over us, and we have suffered so much because of him. So let us kill him so we are no longer troubled by his words. We will not let him be our ruler, because it is our right as the older brothers to rule over this people.
I do not write on these plates everything they said against me. But it is enough to say that they tried to take my life.
The Lord warned me that I, Nephi, should leave them and flee into the wilderness, along with everyone who would go with me.

So I, Nephi, took my family, and also Zoram and his family, and Sam my older brother and his family, and Jacob and Joseph my younger brothers, and also my sisters, and everyone who would go with me. Those who came with me were the ones who believed in the warnings and revelations of God, and so they listened to my words.
We took our tents and whatever we could carry, and traveled through the wilderness for many days. After journeying for many days, we set up camp.
My people wanted to name the place Nephi, so we called it Nephi.
All those who were with me began calling themselves the people of Nephi.
We were careful to keep the judgments, the statutes, and the commandments of the Lord in all things, according to the law of Moses.
The Lord was with us, and we prospered greatly. We planted seeds and harvested abundantly. We began raising flocks, herds, and animals of every kind.
I, Nephi, had also brought the records engraved on the plates of brass, and the ball, or compass, which the Lord had prepared for my father, as has already been written.
We began to prosper greatly and to multiply in the land.

I, Nephi, took the sword of Laban and made many swords patterned after it, in case the people now called Lamanites should attack and try to destroy us. For I knew how much they hated me, my children, and my people.
I taught my people to build buildings and to work with all kinds of materials—wood, iron, copper, brass, steel, gold, silver, and precious ores, which were found in great abundance.

I, Nephi, built a temple. I designed it after the pattern of the temple of Solomon, though it was not built with as many precious materials because they could not be found in the land. So it could not match Solomon's temple in that way. But the design of the building followed the temple of Solomon, and the workmanship was extremely fine.
I, Nephi, encouraged my people to be hard-working and to labor with their hands.
They wanted me to be their king. But I, Nephi, did not want them to have a king. Even so, I did everything in my power for them.

The words of the Lord to my brothers had been fulfilled—He had said that I would be their ruler and teacher. And I had been their ruler and teacher, according to the Lord's commandments, until they tried to take my life.
The word of the Lord was fulfilled which He spoke to me, saying: If they will not listen to your words, they will be cut off from the Lord's presence. And indeed, they were cut off from His presence.

He had caused a curse to come upon them—a terrible curse—because of their wickedness. They had hardened their hearts against Him until they became like stone. So, because they had been light-skinned and very beautiful and attractive, and so that they would not be appealing to my people, the Lord God caused a dark skin to come upon them.
And the Lord God said: I will make them unappealing to your people unless they repent of their wickedness.
And cursed will be anyone who intermarries with them, for they will receive the same curse. The Lord said it, and it was done.
Because of the curse upon them, they became a lazy people, full of mischief and cunning, and they hunted wild animals in the wilderness.

The Lord God said to me: They will be a constant challenge to your descendants, to remind them of me. And if your descendants do not remember me and listen to my words, the Lamanites will afflict them even to the point of destruction.
I, Nephi, set apart Jacob and Joseph to be priests and teachers over the land of my people.
And we lived after the manner of happiness.
Thirty years had passed since we left Jerusalem.
I, Nephi, had kept the records of my people on the plates I had made up to this point.
The Lord God said to me: Make other plates, and you shall engrave many good things on them that I approve of, for the benefit of your people.
So I, Nephi, being obedient to the Lord's commandments, went and made these plates on which I have engraved these things.
I engraved what is pleasing to God. And if my people are pleased with the things of God, they will be pleased with what I have engraved on these plates.
If my people want to know the more detailed history of my people, they must look at my other plates.
It is enough to say that forty years had passed, and we had already experienced wars and conflicts with our brothers.

6.text: These are the words of Jacob, the brother of Nephi, which he spoke to the people of Nephi:

My beloved brothers and sisters, I, Jacob, having been called by God and ordained according to His holy order, and having been set apart by my brother Nephi—whom you look to as a king or protector and depend on for safety—you know that I have spoken many things to you.

Nevertheless, I speak to you again because I care deeply about the welfare of your souls. My concern for you is great, and you know it always has been. I have urged you with all my effort. I have taught you the words of my father. I have spoken to you about everything that has been written, from the creation of the world.

Now I want to speak to you about things that are happening now and things that are yet to come. So I will read to you the words of Isaiah. These are the words my brother Nephi wanted me to share with you. I speak to you for your benefit, so that you may learn and glorify the name of your God.

The words I will read are what Isaiah spoke about the entire house of Israel. They can be applied to you, because you are part of the house of Israel. Many things spoken by Isaiah can be applied to you, because you belong to the house of Israel.

Here are the words: The Lord God says: I will raise my hand to the Gentiles and set up my banner for the people. They will bring your sons in their arms, and your daughters will be carried on their shoulders.

Kings will be your caretakers, and their queens will be your nurturers. They will bow down to you with their faces to the ground and lick the dust of your feet. Then you will know that I am the Lord, for those who wait for me will not be put to shame.
Now I, Jacob, want to say something about these words. The Lord has shown me that the people who were at Jerusalem, where we came from, have been killed and carried away as captives.

Nevertheless, the Lord has shown me that they will return again. He has also shown me that the Lord God, the Holy One of Israel, will appear to them in the flesh. And after He appears to them, they will whip Him and crucify Him, according to the words of the angel who told me.

After they have hardened their hearts and stiffened their necks against the Holy One of Israel, the judgments of the Holy One of Israel will come upon them. The day will come when they will be struck down and afflicted.

After they are driven back and forth—for so the angel said—many will suffer physically but will not be allowed to perish, because of the prayers of the faithful. They will be scattered, attacked, and hated. Yet the Lord will be merciful to them, and when they come to know their Redeemer, they will be gathered together again to the lands of their inheritance.

Blessed are the Gentiles about whom the prophet has written. For if they repent, do not fight against Zion, and do not join that great and corrupt church, they will be saved. For the Lord God will keep the covenants He has made with His children, and that is why the prophet wrote these things.

Those who fight against Zion and the covenant people of the Lord will be humiliated. The people of the Lord will not be put to shame, for the people of the Lord are those who wait for Him. They still await the coming of the Messiah.

According to the words of the prophet, the Messiah will reach out a second time to gather them. He will show Himself to them in power and great glory, destroying their enemies, when the day comes that they believe in Him. He will not destroy anyone who believes in Him.
Those who do not believe in Him will be destroyed by fire, storms, earthquakes, wars, disease, and famine. And they will know that the Lord is God, the Holy One of Israel.
Can the captive be taken from a mighty warrior, or the prisoner of a tyrant be set free?

But the Lord says: Even the captives of the mighty will be taken away, and the prisoners of the terrible will be rescued, for the Mighty God will deliver His covenant people. For the Lord says: I will fight against those who fight against you—

And I will make those who oppress you consume their own flesh, and they will be drunk with their own blood as if it were wine. And all people will know that I, the Lord, am your Savior and your Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob.

7.text: Yes, the Lord says this: "Have I put you away, or have I cast you off forever? Where is your mother's certificate of divorce? To which creditor have I sold you? You sold yourselves because of your sins, and your mother was put away because of your disobedience."

"When I came, why was no one there? When I called, why did no one answer? O house of Israel, is my hand too short to redeem? Do I have no power to deliver? When I rebuke the sea, it dries up. I make rivers into a wilderness, and their fish rot because there is no water. They die of thirst."
"I clothe the heavens with darkness and make sackcloth their covering."
The Lord God has given me the ability to speak the right word at the right time to you, O house of Israel. When you are weary, he wakes me morning by morning. He opens my ear to hear and learn.
The Lord God has opened my ear, and I was not rebellious. I did not turn away.
I gave my back to those who struck me and my cheeks to those who pulled out my hair. I did not hide my face from shame and spitting.
The Lord God will help me, so I will not be disgraced. I have set my face like flint, and I know I will not be put to shame.
The Lord is near, and he vindicates me. Who will oppose me? Let us stand together. Who is my adversary? Let him come near, and I will strike him with the strength of my mouth.
The Lord God will help me. All those who condemn me will wear out like a garment — the moth will eat them up.
Who among you fears the Lord and obeys the voice of his servant, yet walks in darkness and has no light?

All of you who kindle your own fire and surround yourselves with sparks — go ahead and walk in the light of your own fire and the sparks you have lit. But this is what you will receive from my hand: you will lie down in sorrow.

8.text: "Listen to me, you who pursue righteousness. Look to the rock from which you were cut and to the quarry from which you were dug."
"Look to Abraham your father and to Sarah who gave birth to you. I called him when he was alone, and I blessed him."

"The Lord will comfort Zion. He will comfort all her ruined places. He will make her wilderness like Eden and her desert like the garden of the Lord. Joy and gladness will be found there — thanksgiving and the sound of singing."
"Listen to me, my people. Pay attention, my nation. A law will go out from me, and I will establish my justice as a light for the people."
"My righteousness is near. My salvation has gone forth. My arm will judge the nations. The islands will wait for me and trust in my power."

"Lift up your eyes to the heavens and look at the earth below. The heavens will vanish like smoke, and the earth will wear out like a garment. Those who live on it will die in the same way. But my salvation will last forever, and my righteousness will never be destroyed."
"Listen to me, you who know what is right — you people who have my law written in your hearts. Do not fear the insults of men or be afraid of their mockery."
"The moth will eat them like a garment, and the worm will eat them like wool. But my righteousness will last forever, and my salvation from generation to generation."
"Awake, awake! Put on strength, O arm of the Lord! Awake as in ancient days! Are you not the one who cut Rahab to pieces and wounded the dragon?"
"Are you not the one who dried up the sea — the waters of the great deep? Who made the bottom of the sea a road for the redeemed to cross?"
"The redeemed of the Lord will return and come to Zion with singing. Everlasting joy and holiness will be on their heads. They will obtain gladness and joy, and sorrow and mourning will flee away."
"I am the one who comforts you. Why are you afraid of mortal man, of human beings who are like grass?"

"You have forgotten the Lord your Maker, who stretched out the heavens and laid the foundations of the earth. You live in constant fear every day because of the fury of the oppressor who seems ready to destroy. But where is the fury of the oppressor?"
"The captive exile will soon be set free. He will not die in the pit, and his food will not run out."
"I am the Lord your God, whose waves roar. The Lord of Hosts is my name."
"I have put my words in your mouth and covered you in the shadow of my hand, so I may plant the heavens and lay the foundations of the earth and say to Zion: 'You are my people.'"
"Awake, awake! Stand up, O Jerusalem! You have drunk from the hand of the Lord the cup of his fury. You have drained the cup of trembling to the very last drop."
"Of all the children she has raised, there is no one to guide her. No one takes her by the hand."
"Two disasters have come upon you — who will feel sorry for you? Destruction and ruin, famine and the sword — who can comfort you?"
"Your children have fainted, except these two. They lie at the head of every street, like a wild bull caught in a net. They are full of the fury of the Lord, the rebuke of your God."
"Listen to this, you who are afflicted — drunk, but not with wine."
"Your Lord, the Lord your God who defends his people, says this: 'I have taken the cup of trembling out of your hand — the dregs of the cup of my fury. You will never drink from it again.'"
"I will put it into the hands of those who tormented you — those who said to you, 'Bow down so we can walk over you,' and you laid your body on the ground like a street for them to walk on."
"Awake, awake! Put on your strength, O Zion! Put on your beautiful garments, O Jerusalem, the holy city! From now on, the unworthy and the unclean will never enter you again."
"Shake off the dust! Rise up! Sit in your place of honor, O Jerusalem! Free yourself from the chains around your neck, O captive daughter of Zion."

9.text: Now, my beloved brothers and sisters, I have read these things so you can understand the covenants the Lord has made with all the house of Israel —

that he has spoken to the Jews through his holy prophets from the very beginning, generation after generation, until the time comes when they will be restored to the true church and fold of God — when they will be gathered home to their promised lands and established in all their lands of promise.
My beloved brothers and sisters, I tell you these things so you can rejoice and lift up your heads forever, because of the blessings the Lord God will give to your children.

I know that many of you have searched hard to understand things to come. So I know that you understand that our physical bodies must eventually waste away and die. Nevertheless, in our bodies we will see God.

Yes, I know that you know he will show himself in a physical body to those at Jerusalem, where we came from. It is fitting that it happen among them, because the great Creator must allow himself to become subject to mankind in the flesh and die for all people, so that all people might become subject to him.

Since death has come upon all people as part of the merciful plan of the great Creator, there must be a power of resurrection. The resurrection must come because of the Fall, and the Fall came because of disobedience. Because mankind fell, they were cut off from the presence of the Lord.

Therefore, the Atonement had to be infinite. If it were not an infinite Atonement, our mortal bodies could never become immortal. The first judgment that came upon mankind would have lasted forever. If that were the case, our bodies would have been left to rot and crumble to dust, never to rise again.

Oh, the wisdom of God, his mercy and grace! If our bodies never rose again, our spirits would become subject to the angel who fell from the presence of the Eternal God and became the devil, never to rise again.

Our spirits would have become like him, and we would become devils — angels serving the devil, shut out from the presence of our God, remaining forever with the father of lies in misery just like him. He is the one who deceived our first parents, who disguises himself as an angel of light, and who stirs up people to form secret conspiracies of murder and all kinds of secret evil.

Oh, how great is the goodness of our God, who prepares a way for us to escape from the grasp of this terrible monster! That monster is death and hell — the death of the body and the death of the spirit.
Because of the way of deliverance provided by our God, the Holy One of Israel, physical death (which is the grave) will give up its dead.

And spiritual death (which is hell) will also give up its dead. Death and hell must release their captives, and the grave must give up the bodies it holds. The bodies and spirits of all people will be reunited by the power of the resurrection of the Holy One of Israel.

Oh, how great is the plan of our God! Paradise will release the spirits of the righteous, and the grave will release their bodies. Spirit and body will be restored to each other, and all people will become immortal — having a perfect knowledge of everything, just as we do now in the flesh, except our knowledge will be perfect.

We will have a perfect knowledge of all our guilt, our impurity, and our exposed condition. And the righteous will have a perfect knowledge of their joy and their righteousness, being clothed with purity — even with the robe of righteousness.

When all people have passed from physical death to life and become immortal, they must appear before the judgment seat of the Holy One of Israel. Then comes the judgment, and they must be judged according to the holy judgment of God.

As surely as the Lord lives — for the Lord God has spoken it, and it is his eternal word that cannot pass away — those who are righteous will remain righteous, and those who are filthy will remain filthy. Those who are filthy are the devil and his angels, and they will go away into everlasting fire prepared for them. Their torment is like a lake of fire and brimstone, whose flame rises up forever and ever and has no end.
Oh, the greatness and the justice of our God! He carries out all his words. They have gone out of his mouth, and his law must be fulfilled.

But the righteous — the saints of the Holy One of Israel — those who have believed in the Holy One of Israel, who have endured the hardships of the world and looked past the shame — they will inherit the kingdom of God, which was prepared for them from the foundation of the world. Their joy will be full forever.

Oh, the greatness of the mercy of our God, the Holy One of Israel! He delivers his saints from that terrible monster — the devil, death, hell, and that lake of fire and brimstone, which is endless torment.
Oh, how great is the holiness of our God! He knows all things. There is nothing that he does not know.

He comes into the world so he can save all people who will listen to his voice. He suffers the pains of all people — the pains of every living person: men, women, and children, who belong to the family of Adam.
He suffers this so that the resurrection can come to all people, so that all can stand before him at the great and judgment day.
He commands all people to repent and be baptized in his name, having perfect faith in the Holy One of Israel, or they cannot be saved in the kingdom of God.
If they will not repent, believe in his name, be baptized in his name, and endure to the end, they must be condemned. The Lord God, the Holy One of Israel, has spoken it.

He has given a law. Where no law has been given, there is no punishment. Where there is no punishment, there is no condemnation. And where there is no condemnation, the mercies of the Holy One of Israel have a claim on those people because of the Atonement, and they are delivered by his power.

The Atonement satisfies the demands of justice for all those who have not received the law, so they are delivered from that terrible monster — death, hell, the devil, and the lake of fire and brimstone, which is endless torment. They are restored to the God who gave them life, who is the Holy One of Israel.

But how terrible it will be for the person who has been given the law — who has received all the commandments of God, just like us — and who breaks them and wastes the time of their probation! Terrible is their condition!

Oh, the cunning plan of the evil one! Oh, how vain and weak and foolish people are! When they are educated, they think they are wise, and they ignore the counsel of God. They push it aside, thinking they already know everything. Their so-called wisdom is foolishness, and it doesn't help them at all. They will be lost.
But being educated is good — if they listen to the counsel of God.

How terrible it will be for the rich — those who are rich in worldly things. Because they are rich, they look down on the poor, persecute the humble, and set their hearts on their wealth. Their treasure is their god. And their treasure will be destroyed along with them.
How terrible for the deaf who will not hear — they will be lost.
How terrible for the blind who will not see — they will be lost too.
How terrible for those whose hearts are hardened, for the knowledge of their sins will strike them at the last day.
How terrible for the liar — he will be cast down to hell.
How terrible for the murderer who kills deliberately — he will die.
How terrible for those who commit sexual sin — they will be cast down to hell.
Yes, how terrible for those who worship idols — the worst of all devils delights in them.
In short, how terrible for all those who die in their sins. They will return to God, see his face, and remain in their sins.

My beloved brothers and sisters, remember how awful it is to sin against that Holy God, and how awful it is to give in to the temptations of the cunning one. Remember: to be focused on worldly desires leads to death, but to be focused on spiritual things leads to eternal life.

My beloved brothers and sisters, listen to my words. Remember the greatness of the Holy One of Israel. Do not say that I have spoken harsh things against you, because if you do, you are rejecting the truth. I have spoken the words of your Maker. I know that the words of truth are hard to hear for anyone living in sin, but the righteous do not fear them because they love the truth and are not shaken by it.

So then, my beloved brothers and sisters, come to the Lord, the Holy One! Remember that his paths are righteous. The way for mankind is narrow, but it lies in a straight path before him. The keeper of the gate is the Holy One of Israel, and he does not use a servant there. There is no other way except through the gate, because he cannot be deceived — for the Lord God is his name.

To anyone who knocks, he will open the door. But the wise, the educated, and the rich who are puffed up because of their learning, their wisdom, and their riches — these are the ones he rejects. Unless they throw these things away and consider themselves fools before God and humble themselves to the very depths, he will not open the door to them.
The things of the wise and the clever will be hidden from them forever — the very happiness that is prepared for the saints.

My beloved brothers and sisters, remember my words. I symbolically take off my garments and shake them before you. I pray to the God of my salvation that he will look at me with his all-seeing eye. At the last day, when all people are judged by their works, you will know that the God of Israel witnessed that I shook your sins from my soul. I stand clean before him and am free of your blood.
My beloved brothers and sisters, turn away from your sins! Shake off the chains of the one who would bind you! Come to that God who is the rock of your salvation.

Prepare your souls for that glorious day when justice will be given to the righteous — the day of judgment. Prepare so you will not shrink back with terrible fear, so you will not remember your guilt perfectly and be forced to cry out: 'Holy, holy are your judgments, O Lord God Almighty! I know my guilt. I broke your law, and my sins are mine. The devil has captured me, and I am trapped in his awful misery.'

But, my brothers and sisters, do I need to wake you up to this terrible reality? Would I be upsetting your souls if your minds were pure? Would I be this blunt with you according to the plainness of the truth if you were free from sin?
If you were holy, I would speak to you about holiness. But since you are not holy, and you look to me as a teacher, I must teach you the consequences of sin.
My soul hates sin, and my heart delights in righteousness. I will praise the holy name of my God.
Come, my brothers and sisters, all of you who are thirsty — come to the waters! You who have no money, come buy and eat! Come buy wine and milk without money and without cost.

Do not spend your money on things that are worthless or your effort on things that cannot satisfy. Listen carefully to me and remember the words I have spoken. Come to the Holy One of Israel and feast on that which never spoils and cannot be corrupted. Let your soul delight in richness.
My beloved brothers and sisters, remember the words of your God. Pray to him constantly during the day and give thanks to his holy name at night. Let your hearts rejoice.

See how great the covenants of the Lord are, and how great his willingness to reach down to the children of men. Because of his greatness, grace, and mercy, he has promised us that our descendants will not be completely destroyed in the flesh. He will preserve them, and in future generations they will become a righteous branch of the house of Israel.
Now, my brothers and sisters, I would say more to you, but tomorrow I will share the rest of my words. Amen.

10.text: Now I, Jacob, speak to you again, my beloved brothers and sisters, about this righteous branch I have been talking about.

The promises we have received apply to us physically. I have been shown that many of our children will die in the flesh because of unbelief. Nevertheless, God will be merciful to many of them, and our children will be restored so they can come to know their true Redeemer.

As I told you, it is necessary that Christ — for last night the angel told me that this would be his name — would come among the Jews, among the most wicked people in the world. They will crucify him, because our God must allow this, and there is no other nation on earth that would crucify their God.
If these same mighty miracles were performed among other nations, they would repent and acknowledge that he is their God.
But because of priestcrafts and wickedness, those at Jerusalem will stiffen their necks against him so that he is crucified.
Because of their wickedness, destruction, famine, disease, and bloodshed will come upon them. Those who are not destroyed will be scattered among all nations.

But the Lord God says: "When the day comes that they believe in me, that I am Christ, then I have promised their ancestors that they will be restored in the flesh, on the earth, to their lands of inheritance."

They will be gathered from their long scattering, from the islands of the sea and from the four corners of the earth. The nations of the Gentiles will be great in my sight, says God, in helping carry them back to their lands of inheritance.

The kings of the Gentiles will be like protective fathers to them, and their queens will be like nurturing mothers. The promises of the Lord are great to the Gentiles, for he has spoken it, and who can argue?
"This land," said God, "will be your land of inheritance, and the Gentiles will be blessed upon this land."
This land will be a land of liberty for the Gentiles, and there will be no kings upon this land who will rise up against the Gentiles.
"I will fortify this land against all other nations."
"Whoever fights against Zion will be destroyed," says God.
"Whoever raises up a king against me will be destroyed. I, the Lord, the King of Heaven, will be their king, and I will be a light to them forever — to all who hear my words."
"To fulfill the covenants I have made with the children of men — what I will do for them while they are in the flesh — I must destroy the secret works of darkness, murder, and abominations."

"Whoever fights against Zion — whether Jew or Gentile, slave or free, male or female — will be destroyed. They are the ones who belong to the wickedness of the whole earth, for those who are not for me are against me," says our God.
"I will fulfill the promises I have made to the children of men — what I will do for them while they are in the flesh."

"Thus says our God: I will afflict your descendants through the Gentiles. Nevertheless, I will soften the hearts of the Gentiles so they will be like a father to them. The Gentiles will be blessed and counted among the house of Israel."

"I will set apart this land for your descendants and those counted among your descendants forever, as their land of inheritance. It is a choice land," God says to me, "above all other lands. I want all who live on it to worship me."

Now, my beloved brothers and sisters, since our merciful God has given us such great knowledge about these things, let us remember him. Let us put aside our sins and not hang our heads in despair, because we are not cast off. We have been driven from our homeland, but we have been led to a better land. The Lord made the sea our path, and we are on an island in the sea.
The Lord's promises are great for those who are on the islands of the sea. Since it says 'islands,' there must be more islands than just this one, and our people live on them too.
The Lord God has led groups away from the house of Israel from time to time, according to his will and purpose. The Lord remembers all those who have been separated, and he remembers us too.
So cheer up! Remember that you are free to choose for yourselves — to choose the way of everlasting death or the way of eternal life.

My beloved brothers and sisters, align yourselves with the will of God and not with the will of the devil and the flesh. Remember that after you are reconciled to God, it is only through the grace of God that you are saved.

May God raise you from death by the power of the resurrection, and also from everlasting death by the power of the Atonement, so you may be received into the eternal kingdom of God, that you may praise him through divine grace. Amen.

11.text: Jacob spoke many more things to my people at that time, but I have only had these things written down, because what I have written is enough for me.

Now I, Nephi, write more of the words of Isaiah, because my soul delights in his words. I will apply his words to my people and send them out to all my children, because Isaiah truly saw my Redeemer, just as I have seen him.

My brother Jacob has also seen the Redeemer as I have seen him. So I will share their words with my children to prove that my words are true. By the testimony of three witnesses, God has said, he will establish his word. He also sends additional witnesses and confirms all his words.

My soul delights in proving to my people the truth of the coming of Christ. This is the reason the law of Moses was given, and everything God has given from the beginning of the world is a symbol pointing to Christ.
My soul also delights in the covenants the Lord has made with our ancestors. My soul delights in his grace, justice, power, and mercy in the great and eternal plan of deliverance from death.
My soul delights in proving to my people that without Christ, all people would be lost.

If there is no Christ, there is no God. And if there is no God, we do not exist, because nothing could have been created. But there is a God, and he is Christ, and he comes in the fullness of his own time.

Now I will write some of the words of Isaiah, so that anyone among my people who reads these words may lift up their hearts and rejoice for all mankind. These are his words, and you may apply them to yourselves and to all people.

12.text: The message that Isaiah, the son of Amoz, saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem:
In the last days, the mountain of the Lord's temple will be established as the highest of the mountains. It will be raised above the hills, and all nations will flow to it.

Many people will come and say, "Let us go up to the mountain of the Lord, to the house of the God of Jacob! He will teach us his ways, and we will walk in his paths." For the law will go out from Zion, and the word of the Lord from Jerusalem.

He will judge between nations and settle disputes for many peoples. They will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks. Nation will not lift up sword against nation, and they will not learn war anymore.
O house of Jacob, come, let us walk in the light of the Lord! Yes, come — for you have all gone astray, each person to their own wicked ways.

Lord, you have abandoned your people, the house of Jacob, because they are filled with influences from the east and listen to fortune-tellers like the Philistines. They delight in the ways of foreigners.
Their land is full of silver and gold, and there is no end to their treasures. Their land is full of horses, and there is no end to their chariots.
Their land is also full of idols. They worship the work of their own hands — what their own fingers have made.
The common person does not bow down, and the great person does not humble himself — do not forgive them.
You wicked ones — go hide in the rocks and bury yourselves in the dust! The fear of the Lord and the glory of his majesty will strike you down.
The proud looks of mankind will be humbled, and human arrogance will be brought low. The Lord alone will be exalted in that day.
The day of the Lord of Hosts is coming soon upon all nations — upon every proud and arrogant person, upon everyone who is lifted up — and they will be brought low.
That day will come upon all the tall cedars of Lebanon and all the oaks of Bashan;
upon all the high mountains and all the hills; upon all the nations that are lifted up, and upon every people;
upon every tall tower and every fortified wall;
upon all the ships of the sea and all the ships of Tarshish, and upon everything beautiful to look at.
Human pride will be brought down, and human arrogance will be made low. The Lord alone will be exalted in that day.
The idols will completely disappear.
People will flee into caves in the rocks and holes in the ground because of the fear of the Lord and the glory of his majesty, when he rises to shake the earth terribly.
In that day, people will throw away their idols of silver and gold — the idols they made for themselves to worship — to the moles and bats.
They will flee into the crevices and cracks of the rocks because of the fear of the Lord and the glory of his majesty, when he rises to shake the earth terribly.
Stop putting your trust in mere humans, who have only a breath in their nostrils. What are they really worth?

13.text: The Lord, the Lord of Hosts, is taking away from Jerusalem and Judah every supply and support — all their supplies of food and water —
the mighty man and the warrior, the judge and the prophet, the wise advisor and the elder,
the military captain, the respected leader, the counselor, the skilled craftsman, and the eloquent speaker.
"I will give them children as their leaders, and young, inexperienced rulers will govern them."
The people will oppress one another — neighbor against neighbor. The young will disrespect the old, and the common will defy the honorable.
A man will grab his own brother from his father's household and say, "You have a coat — you be our leader! Take charge of this mess!"
But that day the man will protest, saying, "I cannot fix this! In my own house there is neither food nor clothing. Do not make me a leader of the people."
Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen, because everything they say and do is against the Lord, provoking his glorious presence.
The look on their faces gives them away. They parade their sin like Sodom and cannot hide it. How terrible for their souls — they have brought evil upon themselves!
Tell the righteous that all will be well with them, for they will enjoy the fruit of their good deeds.
How terrible for the wicked — they will be destroyed! What they have done will be done to them.
As for my people, children are their oppressors and women rule over them. My people, your leaders are leading you astray and turning you from the right path.
The Lord rises to present his case. He stands to judge the people.
The Lord enters into judgment against the elders and leaders of his people: "You have devoured the vineyard! The goods stolen from the poor are in your houses!"
"What gives you the right to crush my people and grind the faces of the poor?" says the Lord God of Hosts.
The Lord also says: "Because the daughters of Zion are proud, walking with outstretched necks and flirtatious eyes, walking with dainty steps and jingling their ankle bracelets —"
the Lord will strike the heads of the daughters of Zion with sores and lay bare their condition.
In that day the Lord will take away their finery: the jingling anklets, the headbands, and the crescent-shaped necklaces;
the pendants, the bracelets, and the veils;
the headdresses, the ankle chains, the sashes, the perfume bottles, and the earrings;
the rings and the nose jewels;
the fine robes, the capes, the cloaks, and the purses;
the mirrors, the linen garments, the turbans, and the shawls.
Instead of perfume there will be a stench; instead of a sash, a rope; instead of styled hair, baldness; instead of fine clothing, sackcloth; and branding instead of beauty.
Your men will fall by the sword and your warriors in battle.
Her gates will mourn and weep. She will sit desolate on the ground.

14.text: In that day, seven women will grab hold of one man and say, "We will provide our own food and our own clothing — just let us be called by your name. Take away our disgrace!"
In that day, the branch of the Lord will be beautiful and glorious. The fruit of the land will be the pride and glory of the survivors of Israel.
Those who are left in Zion, who remain in Jerusalem, will be called holy — everyone who is recorded among the living in Jerusalem.
This will happen when the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and cleansed the bloodstains from Jerusalem by a spirit of judgment and a spirit of burning.

Then the Lord will create over every home on Mount Zion and over her assemblies a cloud of smoke by day and the glow of a flaming fire by night. Over all the glory of Zion there will be a canopy of protection.
There will be a shelter to give shade from the heat by day, and a refuge and covering from the storm and rain.

15.text: Now I will sing a song about my beloved — a song about his vineyard. My beloved had a vineyard on a very fertile hillside.

He dug it up and cleared out the stones. He planted it with the finest vines. He built a watchtower in the middle of it and carved out a winepress. He expected it to produce good grapes, but it produced only wild, worthless grapes.
"Now, you residents of Jerusalem and people of Judah, judge between me and my vineyard."
"What more could I have done for my vineyard than I already did? When I expected good grapes, why did it produce wild ones?"
"Now let me tell you what I will do to my vineyard. I will tear down its hedge, and it will be destroyed. I will break down its wall, and it will be trampled."
"I will make it a wasteland. It will not be pruned or tended. Thorns and briers will grow up in it. I will also command the clouds not to rain on it."

The vineyard of the Lord of Hosts is the house of Israel, and the people of Judah are his cherished garden. He looked for justice, but found only oppression; he looked for righteousness, but heard only cries of distress.
How terrible for those who add house to house and field to field until there is no room left and you live alone in the land!
The Lord of Hosts has declared in my hearing: "Surely many houses will become empty — great and beautiful houses will have no one living in them."
Ten acres of vineyard will produce only six gallons of wine, and a large amount of seed will produce only a small harvest.
How terrible for those who get up early in the morning chasing alcohol, who stay up late at night until wine inflames them!
They have harps and lyres at their feasts, tambourines, flutes, and wine. But they have no regard for what the Lord is doing, and they pay no attention to the work of his hands.
Therefore my people go into exile because they lack understanding. Their leaders are starving, and the masses are dying of thirst.
The grave has enlarged itself and opened its mouth without limit. Down into it will go their splendor, their crowds, their noise, and their revelers.
The common person will be brought down, the important person humbled, and the eyes of the arrogant humbled.
But the Lord of Hosts will be exalted by his justice, and the holy God will show himself holy through his righteousness.
Then lambs will graze as in their own pasture, and wanderers will eat in the ruins of the wealthy.
How terrible for those who drag sin along with cords of deceit, who haul wickedness as if with cart ropes!
They say, "Let God hurry up and do his work so we can see it. Let the plan of the Holy One of Israel come quickly so we can know it."
How terrible for those who call evil good and good evil, who turn darkness into light and light into darkness, who turn bitter into sweet and sweet into bitter!
How terrible for those who are wise in their own eyes and clever in their own sight!
How terrible for those who are heroes at drinking wine and champions at mixing drinks!
They acquit the guilty for a bribe and deny justice to the innocent.

Therefore, as fire consumes straw and as dry grass shrivels in the flame, so their roots will rot and their blossoms will blow away like dust. They have rejected the law of the Lord of Hosts and despised the word of the Holy One of Israel.

That is why the Lord's anger burns against his people. He has raised his hand against them and struck them down. The mountains trembled, and dead bodies lay in the streets like garbage. Despite all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still raised.
He raises a banner for distant nations and whistles for those at the ends of the earth. They come quickly, racing toward him.
Not one of them grows tired or stumbles. None of them slumbers or sleeps. Not a belt is loosened, not a sandal strap is broken.
Their arrows are sharp, and all their bows are strung. Their horses' hooves are hard as flint, and their chariot wheels spin like a whirlwind.
Their roar is like that of a lion. They growl and seize their prey and carry it off with no one to rescue it.
In that day they will roar over their prey like the roaring of the sea. If anyone looks at the land, they will see only darkness and distress — the light is darkened by the clouds.

16.text: In the year that King Uzziah died, I saw the Lord sitting on a throne, high and exalted, and the train of his robe filled the temple.
Above him stood seraphim, each with six wings: with two they covered their faces, with two they covered their feet, and with two they flew.
They called to one another, saying, "Holy, holy, holy is the Lord of Hosts! The whole earth is full of his glory!"
The doorposts shook at the sound of their voices, and the temple was filled with smoke.
Then I cried out, "I am ruined! For I am a man of unclean lips, and I live among a people of unclean lips — and my eyes have seen the King, the Lord of Hosts!"
Then one of the seraphim flew to me with a live coal in his hand, which he had taken with tongs from the altar.
He touched my mouth with it and said, "See, this has touched your lips. Your guilt is taken away and your sin is forgiven."
Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying, "Whom shall I send? Who will go for us?" And I said, "Here I am — send me!"
He said, "Go and tell this people: 'You will hear and hear but not understand. You will see and see but not perceive.'"

"Make the hearts of this people calloused, make their ears dull, and close their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn and be healed."
Then I said, "Lord, how long?" And he answered, "Until the cities lie ruined and without inhabitant, the houses are left deserted, and the land is utterly desolate."
"Until the Lord has sent everyone far away and the land is completely abandoned."
"But a tenth will remain in the land, though it will be laid waste again. Like a terebinth tree or an oak whose stump remains when it is cut down, so the holy seed will be the stump that survives."

17.text: In the days of Ahaz son of Jotham, son of Uzziah, king of Judah, King Rezin of Syria and King Pekah son of Remaliah of Israel marched up to Jerusalem to attack it, but they could not overpower it.
The house of David was told, "Syria has allied with Ephraim." And the hearts of Ahaz and his people trembled, like trees shaking in the wind.
Then the Lord said to Isaiah, "Go out to meet Ahaz, you and your son Shear-Jashub, at the end of the aqueduct of the upper pool, on the road to the launderer's field."

"Say to him: 'Be careful, stay calm, and don't be afraid. Don't lose heart because of these two smoldering stumps of firewood — because of the fierce anger of Rezin and Syria and the son of Remaliah.'"
"Syria, Ephraim, and the son of Remaliah have plotted evil against you, saying:"
"'Let us invade Judah, terrorize it, break through its defenses, and set up the son of Tabeal as king over it.'"
This is what the Lord God says: "It will not happen. It will not come to pass."
"The capital of Syria is Damascus, and the ruler of Damascus is Rezin. Within sixty-five years Ephraim will be shattered and will no longer be a people."
"The capital of Ephraim is Samaria, and the ruler of Samaria is Remaliah's son. If you do not stand firm in your faith, you will not stand at all."
The Lord spoke again to Ahaz, saying:
"Ask the Lord your God for a sign — ask for it either in the deepest depths or in the highest heights."
But Ahaz said, "I will not ask. I will not test the Lord."
Then Isaiah said, "Listen, house of David! Is it not enough that you exhaust the patience of men? Must you exhaust the patience of my God as well?"
"Therefore the Lord himself will give you a sign: A virgin will conceive and bear a son, and she will call his name Immanuel."
He will eat butter and honey until he is old enough to know how to refuse evil and choose good.
Before the child is old enough to refuse evil and choose good, the land of the two kings you dread will be abandoned.
The Lord will bring upon you, your people, and your father's house days unlike anything since the day Ephraim broke away from Judah — he will bring the king of Assyria.
In that day the Lord will whistle for the fly that is at the far end of the rivers of Egypt and for the bee in the land of Assyria.
They will all come and settle in the steep ravines, in the crevices of the rocks, on every thornbush, and at every watering place.
In that day the Lord will use a razor hired from beyond the Euphrates River — the king of Assyria — to shave your head, the hair of your legs, and your beard as well.
In that day, a man will keep alive a young cow and two sheep.
Because of the abundance of milk they give, he will eat butter. Everyone left in the land will eat butter and honey.
In that day, every place where there once were a thousand grapevines worth a thousand pieces of silver will become nothing but briers and thorns.
Men will go there with bows and arrows, because the whole land will be covered with briers and thorns.
As for the hills that once were cultivated, no one will go there for fear of the briers and thorns. They will become places where cattle are turned loose and sheep roam.

18.text: The Lord said to me, "Take a large scroll and write on it with an ordinary pen: Maher-Shalal-Hash-Baz."
I called in reliable witnesses to verify the record — Uriah the priest and Zechariah son of Jeberechiah.
Then I went to the prophetess, and she conceived and gave birth to a son. The Lord said to me, "Name him Maher-Shalal-Hash-Baz."
Before the boy knows how to say 'my father' or 'my mother,' the wealth of Damascus and the plunder of Samaria will be carried away by the king of Assyria.
The Lord spoke to me again, saying:
"Because this people has rejected the gently flowing waters of Shiloah and rejoices in Rezin and the son of Remaliah,"
"the Lord is about to bring against them the mighty floodwaters of the Euphrates River — the king of Assyria in all his glory. It will rise over all its channels and overflow all its banks."
"It will sweep into Judah, swirling over it, rising up to the neck. Its outspread wings will cover the entire breadth of your land, O Immanuel!"
"Band together, you nations, and be shattered! Listen, all you distant lands! Prepare for battle, and be shattered! Prepare for battle, and be shattered!"
"Make your plans, but they will come to nothing. Give your orders, but they will not stand, for God is with us!"
The Lord spoke to me with his strong hand upon me and warned me not to follow the way of this people. He said:
"Do not call everything a conspiracy that this people calls a conspiracy. Do not fear what they fear or be terrified by it."
"The Lord of Hosts is the one you should regard as holy. He is the one you should fear. He is the one you should dread."
"He will be a sanctuary — but also a stone that causes people to stumble and a rock that makes them fall. He will be a trap and a snare for the people of Jerusalem."
"Many of them will stumble and fall, be broken, be snared, and be captured."
"Bind up this testimony. Seal the law among my disciples."
I will wait for the Lord, who is hiding his face from the house of Jacob. I will put my hope in him.
Here I am, along with the children the Lord has given me. We are signs and symbols in Israel from the Lord of Hosts, who dwells on Mount Zion.
When someone tells you, "Consult mediums and fortune-tellers who whisper and mutter" — shouldn't a people seek guidance from their God? Should they consult the dead on behalf of the living?
Look to the law and to the testimony! If what they say does not agree with this, it is because there is no light in them.
They will wander through the land in distress and hunger. When they are hungry, they will become enraged and curse their king and their God, and look upward.
Then they will look toward the earth and see only trouble, darkness, and the gloom of anguish. They will be thrust into utter darkness.

19.text: Nevertheless, the gloom will not last for those who were in anguish. In earlier times he humbled the land of Zebulun and the land of Naphtali, but in the future he will honor the way to the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the nations.
The people walking in darkness have seen a great light. On those living in the land of the shadow of death, a light has shined.
You have enlarged the nation and increased their joy. They rejoice before you as people rejoice at the harvest, as warriors rejoice when dividing the plunder.
For you have shattered the yoke that burdens them, the bar across their shoulders, the rod of their oppressor.
Every warrior's boot used in battle and every garment rolled in blood will be fuel for the fire.
For unto us a child is born, unto us a son is given, and the government will be on his shoulders. He will be called Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace.

Of the increase of his government and peace there will be no end. He will reign on David's throne and over his kingdom, establishing and upholding it with justice and righteousness from that time on and forever. The zeal of the Lord of Hosts will accomplish this.
The Lord sent a message against Jacob, and it came upon Israel.
All the people will know it — Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria — who say with pride and arrogance:
"The bricks have fallen down, but we will rebuild with cut stone. The fig trees have been chopped down, but we will replace them with cedars."
Therefore the Lord has raised up the enemies of Rezin against them and has stirred up their foes.
The Syrians from the east and the Philistines from the west devour Israel with open mouths. Despite all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still raised.
But the people have not returned to the one who struck them, nor have they sought the Lord of Hosts.
So the Lord will cut off from Israel both head and tail, palm branch and reed, in a single day.
The elders and respected leaders are the head; the prophets who teach lies are the tail.
Those who guide this people are leading them astray, and those who are led are being destroyed.

Therefore the Lord takes no pleasure in their young men, and has no compassion on their orphans and widows, because every one of them is godless and wicked, and every mouth speaks foolishness. Despite all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still raised.
Wickedness burns like a fire. It consumes briers and thorns; it sets the forest thickets ablaze, and the smoke columns rise upward.
By the wrath of the Lord of Hosts the land is scorched, and the people are fuel for the fire. No one spares his brother.
