They grab on the right but are still hungry; they eat on the left but are not satisfied. Each one feeds on the flesh of his own people —
Manasseh devours Ephraim, and Ephraim devours Manasseh; together they turn against Judah. Despite all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still raised.

20.text: How terrible for those who make unjust laws and issue oppressive regulations,
to deprive the poor of their rights and take away justice from the needy among my people, making widows their prey and robbing the fatherless!
What will you do on the day of reckoning, when disaster comes from far away? To whom will you run for help? Where will you leave your wealth?
Without me, you will bow down among the prisoners or fall among the slain. Despite all this, his anger has not turned away, and his hand is still raised.
"Assyria — the rod of my anger! The club in their hands is my fury."
"I send him against a godless nation. I order him against a people who anger me — to seize their plunder, to carry off their spoils, and to trample them down like mud in the streets."
"But this is not what Assyria intends, and it is not what he has in mind. His purpose is to destroy and to wipe out many nations."
He says, "Are not all my commanders kings?"
"Is not Calno like Carchemish? Is not Hamath like Arpad? Is not Samaria like Damascus?"
"My hand has reached the kingdoms of idols, whose carved images surpassed those of Jerusalem and Samaria."
"Will I not do to Jerusalem and her idols what I did to Samaria and hers?"
But when the Lord has finished all his work on Mount Zion and Jerusalem, he will say, "I will punish the king of Assyria for his arrogant heart and proud, boastful eyes."

For the king of Assyria says, "By the strength of my hand I have done this, and by my wisdom — because I am so clever. I removed the boundaries of nations, I plundered their treasures, and like a mighty warrior I brought their kings to their knees."
"My hand reached into the wealth of nations as into a nest. I gathered all the earth as one gathers abandoned eggs. Not a wing fluttered, not a mouth opened, not a sound was made."

Does the axe boast against the one who swings it? Does the saw exalt itself above the one who uses it? That would be like a rod shaking the person who lifts it, or a staff lifting up the one who is not wood!
Therefore, the Lord, the Lord of Hosts, will send a wasting disease on his sturdy warriors. Under his glory, a fire will be kindled.
The Light of Israel will become a fire, and his Holy One a flame. In a single day it will burn and consume his thorns and briers.
The splendor of his forests and fertile fields it will destroy completely, both body and soul, as when a sick man wastes away.
The remaining trees of his forests will be so few that a child could count them.
In that day, the remnant of Israel, the survivors of the house of Jacob, will no longer rely on the one who struck them. They will truly rely on the Lord, the Holy One of Israel.
A remnant will return — a remnant of Jacob — to the Mighty God.
Though your people Israel are as numerous as the sand by the sea, only a remnant will return. Destruction has been decreed, and it will overflow with righteousness.
For the Lord God of Hosts will carry out the destruction decreed upon the whole land.
Therefore this is what the Lord God of Hosts says: "My people who live in Zion, do not be afraid of the Assyrians. They may beat you with a rod and lift up their staff against you, as Egypt did."
"In just a very short time, my anger against you will end, and my wrath will turn to their destruction."
The Lord of Hosts will strike them with a whip, as when he struck Midian at the rock of Oreb. He will raise his staff over the waters, as he did in Egypt.
In that day, their burden will be lifted from your shoulders and their yoke from your neck. The yoke will be broken because of the anointing.
The enemy has come to Aiath, passed through Migron, and stored his supplies at Michmash.
They have crossed the pass and camped at Geba. Ramah trembles with fear. Gibeah of Saul has fled.
Cry out, daughter of Gallim! Listen, Laishah! Poor Anathoth!
Madmenah is in flight. The people of Gebim scramble to escape.
This very day he will halt at Nob. He will shake his fist at the mountain of the daughter of Zion, the hill of Jerusalem.
See, the Lord, the Lord of Hosts, will lop off the branches with terrifying power. The tall ones will be cut down, the lofty will be laid low.
He will cut down the forest thickets with an axe, and Lebanon will fall before the Mighty One.

21.text: A shoot will come up from the stump of Jesse, and a branch will grow from his roots.
The Spirit of the Lord will rest on him — the spirit of wisdom and understanding, the spirit of counsel and might, the spirit of knowledge and the fear of the Lord.
He will delight in the fear of the Lord. He will not judge by what he sees with his eyes or decide by what he hears with his ears.

Instead, with righteousness he will judge the poor, and with fairness he will decide for the humble of the earth. He will strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he will slay the wicked.
Righteousness will be his belt, and faithfulness the sash around his waist.
The wolf will live with the lamb, and the leopard will lie down with the young goat. The calf and the lion and the fattened calf will be together, and a little child will lead them.
The cow will graze with the bear. Their young will lie down together. The lion will eat straw like the ox.
An infant will play near the hole of a cobra, and a young child will put his hand into the viper's den.
They will not hurt or destroy in all my holy mountain, for the earth will be full of the knowledge of the Lord as the waters cover the sea.
In that day, the root of Jesse will stand as a banner for the peoples. The nations will rally to him, and his resting place will be glorious.
In that day the Lord will reach out his hand a second time to reclaim the remnant of his people — from Assyria, Egypt, Pathros, Cush, Elam, Shinar, Hamath, and the islands of the sea.
He will raise a banner for the nations and gather the exiles of Israel. He will collect the scattered people of Judah from the four corners of the earth.
Ephraim's jealousy will disappear, and Judah's enemies will be cut off. Ephraim will not be jealous of Judah, and Judah will not be hostile toward Ephraim.

Together they will swoop down on the slopes of Philistia to the west. Together they will plunder the people to the east. They will take possession of Edom and Moab, and the Ammonites will be subject to them.

The Lord will completely dry up the gulf of the Egyptian sea. With a scorching wind he will sweep his hand over the Euphrates River and split it into seven streams so that people can cross over in sandals.
There will be a highway for the remnant of his people that is left from Assyria, just as there was for Israel when they came up out of Egypt.

22.text: In that day you will say: "I will praise you, Lord! Although you were angry with me, your anger has turned away, and you have comforted me."
"God is my salvation! I will trust and not be afraid. The Lord Jehovah is my strength and my song — he has become my salvation."
"With joy you will draw water from the wells of salvation."
In that day you will say: "Give praise to the Lord! Call on his name! Make known among the nations what he has done. Proclaim that his name is exalted."
"Sing to the Lord, for he has done glorious things! Let this be known in all the earth."
"Shout aloud and sing for joy, people of Zion, for great is the Holy One of Israel among you!"

23.text: A prophecy about Babylon that Isaiah son of Amoz received:
"Raise a banner on a bare hilltop! Shout to them! Wave your hand for them to enter the gates of the nobles."
"I have commanded my holy warriors. I have called my mighty men to carry out my anger — those who rejoice in my triumph."
"Listen! A noise on the mountains — like that of a great multitude! Listen! An uproar among the kingdoms — nations gathering together! The Lord of Hosts is mustering an army for battle."
"They come from faraway lands, from the ends of the heavens — the Lord and the weapons of his wrath — to destroy the whole country."
"Wail, for the day of the Lord is near! It will come as destruction from the Almighty."
"Every hand will go limp, and every heart will melt."
"Terror will seize them. Pain and anguish will grip them. They will writhe like a woman in labor. They will look at each other in horror, their faces red as flames."
"The day of the Lord is coming — a cruel day of wrath and fierce anger — to make the land desolate and destroy the sinners within it."
"The stars of heaven and their constellations will not show their light. The sun will be dark when it rises, and the moon will not shine."
"I will punish the world for its evil and the wicked for their sins. I will put an end to the arrogance of the proud and humble the boasting of the ruthless."
"I will make people scarcer than pure gold — rarer than the gold of Ophir."
"I will shake the heavens, and the earth will be knocked from its place by the wrath of the Lord of Hosts, in the day of his fierce anger."
"Like a hunted gazelle, like sheep without a shepherd, each person will flee to their own people and run to their own land."
"Whoever is captured will be run through with a sword. Whoever is caught will fall by the sword."
"Their infants will be dashed to pieces before their eyes. Their homes will be plundered and their wives violated."
"I will stir up the Medes against them — a people who do not care about silver and have no delight in gold."
"Their bows will strike down the young men. They will have no mercy on infants and will show no compassion for children."
"Babylon, the jewel of kingdoms, the glory and pride of the Babylonians, will be overthrown by God like Sodom and Gomorrah."
"It will never be inhabited or lived in from generation to generation. No nomad will pitch a tent there; no shepherd will rest his flock there."
"Wild animals will lie there. Their houses will be full of howling creatures. Owls will dwell there, and wild goats will leap about."

"Jackals will howl in her fortresses and wild dogs in her luxurious palaces. Her time is near, and her days will not be prolonged. For I will destroy her swiftly; yes, I will be merciful to my people, but the wicked will perish."

24.text: For the Lord will have mercy on Jacob and will still choose Israel and settle them in their own land. Strangers will join them, and they will unite with the house of Jacob.

People will take them and bring them to their homeland—yes, from distant places to the ends of the earth—and they will return to their promised lands. The house of Israel will possess them, and in the Lord's land there will be servants and handmaids. They will take captive those who once held them captive, and they will rule over those who once oppressed them.
In that day, the Lord will give you rest from your sorrow, from your fear, and from the harsh slavery you were forced to endure.
In that day, you will speak this proverb against the king of Babylon and say: How the oppressor has come to an end! The golden city has ceased!
The Lord has broken the staff of the wicked and the scepters of the rulers.
The one who struck the people in anger with endless blows, the one who ruled the nations in fury, is now pursued, and no one holds back.
The whole earth is at rest and quiet; they break out in singing.
Even the pine trees and the cedars of Lebanon rejoice over you, saying: Since you have fallen, no one comes to cut us down.

The realm of the dead below is stirred up to meet you when you arrive. It wakes up the spirits of the dead for you—all the former leaders of the earth. It raises all the kings of the nations from their thrones.
They will all speak and say to you: Have you also become as weak as us? Have you become like us?
Your pride has been brought down to the grave. The sound of your stringed instruments is no longer heard. Worms are spread beneath you, and worms cover you.
How you have fallen from heaven, O Lucifer, son of the morning! You have been cut down to the ground, you who once weakened the nations!
For you said in your heart: I will rise up into heaven. I will raise my throne above the stars of God. I will sit on the mountain of the assembly, in the far reaches of the north.
I will rise above the tops of the clouds. I will be like the Most High.
Yet you will be brought down to hell, to the depths of the pit.
Those who see you will stare at you and think about you, saying: Is this the man who made the earth tremble and shook kingdoms?
Is this the one who turned the world into a wasteland, destroyed its cities, and never released his prisoners?
All the kings of the nations—every one of them—lie in honor, each in his own tomb.

But you are thrown out of your grave like a worthless branch, buried among the remains of those who were killed, pierced by the sword, cast down to the stones of the pit like a body trampled underfoot.
You will not be buried with them, because you destroyed your land and killed your people. The descendants of evildoers will never be honored.
Prepare a place of destruction for his children because of the sins of their fathers, so that they do not rise up, take over the land, or fill the world with their cities.
For I will rise up against them, says the Lord of Hosts, and I will cut off from Babylon its name, its survivors, its children, and its grandchildren, says the Lord.
I will also turn it into a swamp for birds and pools of water. I will sweep it away with the broom of destruction, says the Lord of Hosts.
The Lord of Hosts has sworn, saying: Surely, just as I have planned, so it will happen; and just as I have purposed, so it will stand—
I will crush the Assyrian in my land and trample him on my mountains. Then his yoke will be removed from my people, and his burden will be lifted from their shoulders.
This is the plan that is determined for the whole earth, and this is the hand that is stretched out over all nations.
For the Lord of Hosts has made His plan, and who can stop it? His hand is stretched out, and who can turn it back?
In the year that King Ahaz died, this message was given.
Do not rejoice, all you Philistines, just because the rod that struck you is broken. For from the serpent's root will come a viper, and its offspring will be a fiery flying serpent.
The poorest of the poor will find food, and the needy will lie down in safety. But I will kill your root with famine, and your survivors will be destroyed.
Wail, O gate! Cry out, O city! You, all of Palestina, are dissolved! For from the north comes a cloud of smoke, and no one breaks rank in its formations.
What answer will be given to the messengers of the nations? That the Lord has established Zion, and the poor of His people will find refuge in it.

25.text: Now I, Nephi, will speak about the words I have written, which were spoken by Isaiah. Isaiah said many things that were hard for many of my people to understand, because they do not know about the way the Jews prophesied.
I, Nephi, have not taught my people much about the customs of the Jews, because the things they did were works of darkness and their actions were abominable.
Therefore I write to my people and to all who will receive these words in the future, so they may know the judgments of God — that they come upon all nations, just as he has said.

Listen, my people of the house of Israel, and pay attention to my words. Because Isaiah's words are not easy for you to understand, they are still clear to everyone who is filled with the spirit of prophecy. But I will give you a prophecy according to the spirit in me. I will prophesy with the plainness that has been with me since I left Jerusalem with my father, because my soul delights in plainness for my people, so they can learn.

My soul delights in the words of Isaiah, because I came from Jerusalem and my eyes have seen the customs of the Jews. I know the Jews understand what the prophets said — no other people understand them as well, unless they are taught in the way of the Jews.

But I, Nephi, have not taught my children in the ways of the Jews. However, I myself lived in Jerusalem, so I know about the surrounding regions. I have told my children about the judgments of God that have come upon the Jews, according to what Isaiah said, but I did not write them all down.

I will continue with my own prophecy, in my own plain way — a way in which no one can make mistakes. When Isaiah's prophecies are fulfilled, people will know for certain, at the time they come to pass.

Therefore Isaiah's words are valuable to all people. Whoever thinks they are not — to them I speak specifically and direct my words to my own people. I know his words will be of great value to them in the last days, because in that day they will understand them. That is why I have written them — for their benefit.

As one generation of Jews has been destroyed because of wickedness, they have been destroyed generation after generation according to their sins. None of them were destroyed without being warned by the prophets of the Lord.

They were told about the destruction that would come upon them right after my father left Jerusalem. Yet they hardened their hearts, and according to my prophecy they have been destroyed — except those who were carried away captive to Babylon.
I say this because of the spirit within me. Even though they were carried away, they will return again and possess the land of Jerusalem. They will be restored to the land of their inheritance.

But they will have wars and rumors of wars. When the day comes that the Only Begotten of the Father — the Father of heaven and earth — shows himself to them in the flesh, they will reject him because of their wickedness, hard hearts, and stiff necks.

They will crucify him. After he is laid in a tomb for three days, he will rise from the dead with healing in his wings. All who believe in his name will be saved in the kingdom of God. My soul delights in prophesying about him, for I have seen his day, and my heart glorifies his holy name.

After the Messiah has risen from the dead and shown himself to his people — to all who will believe in his name — Jerusalem will be destroyed again. How terrible for those who fight against God and his people!
The Jews will be scattered among all nations. Babylon will also be destroyed. The Jews will be scattered by other nations.

After they have been scattered and the Lord God has punished them through other nations for many generations — even from generation to generation — until they are persuaded to believe in Christ, the Son of God, and in the Atonement, which is infinite for all mankind, then when that day comes that they believe in Christ and worship the Father with pure hearts and clean hands, and no longer look for another Messiah — then the day will come that they must believe these things.
The Lord will reach out his hand a second time to restore his people from their lost and fallen state. He will do a marvelous work and a wonder among the children of men.

He will bring forth his words to them — words that will judge them at the last day. These words are given to convince them of the true Messiah, who was rejected by them, and to convince them that they need not look for another Messiah to come. There is only one Messiah spoken of by the prophets, and that Messiah is the one who would be rejected by the Jews.
According to the prophets, the Messiah comes six hundred years after my father left Jerusalem. According to the prophets and the angel of God, his name will be Jesus Christ, the Son of God.

Now, my brothers and sisters, I have spoken plainly so you cannot make a mistake. As the Lord God lives — who brought Israel out of Egypt and gave Moses power to heal the people after they were bitten by poisonous snakes (if they would look at the bronze serpent he raised before them) and also gave him power to strike the rock so water came forth — as these things are true and as the Lord God lives, there is no other name given under heaven except Jesus Christ, of whom I have spoken, by which anyone can be saved.

For this reason, the Lord God has promised me that these things I write will be kept, preserved, and handed down to my descendants from generation to generation, so the promise to Joseph may be fulfilled — that his descendants would never perish as long as the earth stands.

These writings will pass from generation to generation as long as the earth stands, according to the will and purpose of God. The nations who possess them will be judged by the words that are written.
We work hard to write, to persuade our children and our people to believe in Christ and to be reconciled to God. For we know that it is by grace that we are saved, after all we can do.
Even though we believe in Christ, we keep the law of Moses and look forward with steadfastness to Christ, until the law is fulfilled.
This is why the law was given. The law has become dead to us, and we are made alive in Christ because of our faith. Yet we keep the law because of the commandments.

We talk of Christ, we rejoice in Christ, we preach of Christ, we prophesy of Christ, and we write according to our prophecies, so that our children may know where to look for the forgiveness of their sins.

We teach about the law so our children may understand that the law is dead. By knowing the law is dead, they can look forward to the life that is in Christ and understand why the law was given. After the law is fulfilled in Christ, they need not harden their hearts against him when the law is no longer needed.

Now my people, you are a stubborn people. That is why I have spoken plainly to you, so you cannot misunderstand. The words I have spoken will stand as a testimony against you, because they are sufficient to teach anyone the right way. The right way is to believe in Christ and not deny him. By denying him, you deny the prophets and the law.

The right way is to believe in Christ and not deny him. Christ is the Holy One of Israel. You must bow before him and worship him with all your might, mind, strength, and whole soul. If you do this, you will never be cast out.
As far as it is necessary, you must keep the rituals and ordinances of God until the law given through Moses is fulfilled.

26.text: After Christ has risen from the dead, he will show himself to you, my children and my beloved people. The words he speaks to you will become the law you should follow.
I have seen that many generations will pass away, and there will be great wars and conflicts among my people.

After the Messiah comes, signs will be given to my people of his birth, death, and resurrection. That will be a great and terrible day for the wicked, because they will be destroyed. They will be destroyed because they cast out the prophets and saints, and stoned them and killed them. The cry of the blood of the saints will rise up to God from the ground against them.
All who are proud and do wickedly — the coming day will burn them up, says the Lord of Hosts. They will be like stubble.

Those who kill the prophets and saints will be swallowed up by the earth, says the Lord of Hosts. Mountains will cover them, whirlwinds will carry them away, and buildings will fall on them and crush them to pieces.

They will be struck with thunder, lightning, earthquakes, and all kinds of destruction. The fire of the Lord's anger will be kindled against them. They will be like stubble, and the coming day will consume them, says the Lord of Hosts.

Oh, the pain and anguish of my soul for the loss of my people who will be killed! I, Nephi, have seen it, and it nearly destroys me in the Lord's presence. But I must cry to my God: Your ways are just.
But the righteous who listen to the prophets and do not destroy them, who look forward to Christ with steadfastness for the signs that are given, despite all persecution — they will not be destroyed.
The Son of Righteousness will appear to them and heal them. They will have peace with him for three generations, and many of the fourth generation will also live in righteousness.

After these things have passed, rapid destruction will come to my people. Despite the pain of my soul, I have seen it. I know it will happen. They sell themselves for nothing. Because of their pride and foolishness, they will reap destruction. Because they give in to the devil and choose works of darkness rather than light, they must go down to hell.
The Spirit of the Lord will not always strive with mankind. When the Spirit stops striving with them, rapid destruction comes. This grieves my soul.
As I spoke about convincing the Jews that Jesus is the Christ, the Gentiles must also be convinced that Jesus is the Christ, the Eternal God.

He reveals himself to all who believe in him, by the power of the Holy Ghost — to every nation, people, and language — working mighty miracles, signs, and wonders among the children of men according to their faith.
I prophesy to you about the last days — about the days when the Lord God will bring these things to pass among the children of men.

After my descendants and my brothers' descendants have dwindled in unbelief and been attacked by the Gentiles — after the Lord God has besieged them on every side with armies and forts — after they have been brought down low in the dust until they are no more — the words of the righteous will still be written, the prayers of the faithful will be heard, and all those who dwindled in unbelief will not be forgotten.

Those who have been destroyed will speak to them from the ground. Their speech will come up from the dust, and their voice will be like one who speaks from beyond the grave. The Lord God will give power to whisper about them from the ground, and their words will whisper from the dust.

The Lord God says: "They will write down the things that happen among them. These things will be written and sealed up in a book. Those who have dwindled in unbelief will not have access to them, because they seek to destroy the things of God."
Those who have been destroyed were destroyed quickly. The multitude of their terrible ones will disappear like chaff that blows away. It will happen in an instant, suddenly, says the Lord God.
Those who have dwindled in unbelief will be struck by the hand of the Gentiles.

The Gentiles are lifted up in the pride of their eyes. They have stumbled because of the greatness of their stumbling block — they have built up many churches. Yet they dismiss the power and miracles of God and preach their own wisdom and learning so they can profit and grind down the poor.
Many churches are built up that cause envy, conflict, and hatred.

There are also secret conspiracies, just as in ancient times, following the devil's pattern. He is the founder of all these things — the founder of murder and works of darkness. He leads people by the neck with a thin cord until he binds them with his strong chains forever.
My beloved brothers and sisters, I tell you that the Lord God does not work in darkness.

He does nothing unless it is for the benefit of the world. He loves the world so much that he lays down his own life to draw all people to him. He does not forbid anyone from partaking of his salvation.
Does he cry to anyone, saying, 'Go away from me'? No! He says: 'Come to me, all you ends of the earth. Buy milk and honey without money and without price.'
Has he commanded anyone to leave the churches or houses of worship? No.
Has he commanded anyone not to partake of his salvation? No! He has given it freely to all people. He has commanded his people to persuade all men to repent.
Has the Lord commanded anyone not to partake of his goodness? No! All people are equally privileged, and none are forbidden.

He commands that there be no priestcrafts. Priestcrafts are when men preach and set themselves up as a light to the world to get gain and praise from the world, but they do not seek the welfare of Zion.

The Lord has forbidden this. Therefore, the Lord God has commanded that all people should have charity, which is love. If they do not have charity, they are nothing. If they have charity, they would not let the laborer in Zion perish.
The laborer in Zion must labor for Zion. If they labor for money, they will perish.

The Lord God has also commanded that people should not murder, lie, steal, take the name of the Lord their God in vain, envy, have malice, argue with one another, or commit sexual sin. Whoever does these things will perish.

None of these sins come from the Lord. He does what is good among the children of men. He does nothing unless it is plain and clear to people. He invites all to come to him and partake of his goodness. He turns no one away who comes to him — black and white, slave and free, male and female. He remembers those in every nation. All are alike unto God, both Jew and Gentile.

27.text: In the last days, in the days of the Gentiles — all the nations of the Gentiles and also the Jews, both those who come to this land and those on other lands, even all the lands of the earth — they will be drunk with wickedness and all kinds of abominations.
When that day comes, they will be visited by the Lord of Hosts with thunder, earthquake, great noise, storm, tempest, and the flame of devouring fire.

All the nations that fight against Zion and cause her distress will be like a dream in the night. It will be like a hungry man who dreams he is eating, but wakes up and his stomach is empty. Or like a thirsty man who dreams he is drinking, but wakes up faint and still thirsty. This is how it will be for all the nations that fight against Mount Zion.
All who do evil — stop and be amazed! You will cry out and weep. You will be drunk, but not from wine. You will stagger, but not from strong drink.
The Lord has poured out upon you the spirit of deep sleep. You have closed your eyes and rejected the prophets. He has covered your rulers and seers because of your wickedness.
The Lord God will bring forth the words of a book to you — the words of those who have died.
The book will be sealed. In the book there will be a revelation from God, from the beginning of the world to the end.
Because of the things that are sealed, the sealed portions will not be delivered during a time of wickedness and abomination. The book will be kept from them.
But the book will be delivered to a man, and he will deliver the words of the book — the words of those who have died in the dust — and he will deliver these words to another person.

But the sealed words he will not deliver, nor will he give away the book. The book will be sealed by the power of God, and the sealed revelation will be kept in the book until the Lord's own time, when they may come forth. They reveal all things from the foundation of the world to the end.

The day is coming when the sealed words of the book will be read from the housetops. They will be read by the power of Christ. All things will be revealed to the children of men — everything that has ever been among them, and everything that ever will be, until the end of the earth.

When the book is delivered to the man I have spoken of, it will be hidden from the world. No one will see it except three witnesses who will see it by the power of God, besides the man to whom the book will be given. They will testify to the truth of the book and the things in it.

No one else will see it except a few others according to God's will, to bear testimony of his word to the children of men. The Lord God has said that the words of the faithful will speak as if from the dead.
The Lord God will proceed to bring forth the words of the book. He will establish his word through as many witnesses as he sees fit. How terrible for anyone who rejects the word of God!
The Lord God will say to the man to whom he delivers the book: "Take these unsealed words and deliver them to another person, so he may show them to a scholar, saying, 'Read this, please.'"
The scholar will say this because of worldly glory and to get gain — not for the glory of God.
And the man will say, "I cannot bring the book because it is sealed."
Then the scholar will say, "I cannot read it."
Then the Lord God will deliver the book and its words again to one who is not a scholar. The unlearned man will say, "I am not a scholar."
Then the Lord God will say to him: "The scholars will not read them, because they have rejected them. I am able to do my own work. Therefore you will read the words I give you."
"Do not touch the sealed portions, because I will bring them forth in my own time. I will show the children of men that I am able to do my own work."

"When you have read the words I have commanded and obtained the witnesses I have promised, you will seal up the book again and hide it for me, so I may preserve the words you have not read, until I see fit in my own wisdom to reveal all things to the children of men."
"For I am God. I am a God of miracles. I will show the world that I am the same yesterday, today, and forever. I work among the children of men only according to their faith."
It will also come to pass that the Lord will say to the man who reads the words:
"Because this people draws near to me with their mouths and honors me with their lips, but their hearts are far from me, and their worship of me is based on rules taught by men —"
"Therefore I will do a marvelous work among this people — a marvelous work and a wonder! The wisdom of their wise men will perish, and the understanding of their clever ones will vanish."

How terrible for those who try to hide their plans from the Lord! They do their works in the dark and say, 'Who sees us? Who knows?' They turn things upside down! But the Lord of Hosts says: 'I will show them that I know all their works. Shall the thing that is made say of its maker, "He did not make me"? Shall the thing that is formed say of the one who formed it, "He had no understanding"?'
But the Lord of Hosts says: "In a very short time Lebanon will be turned into a fertile field, and the fertile field will seem like a forest."
"In that day the deaf will hear the words of the book, and out of gloom and darkness the eyes of the blind will see."
"The humble will increase, and their joy will be in the Lord. The poor among mankind will rejoice in the Holy One of Israel."
"As surely as the Lord lives, they will see that the ruthless will be brought to nothing, the mocker will disappear, and everyone who watches for an opportunity to do evil will be cut off —"
"those who convict a person on the basis of a single word, who set traps for the one who speaks up for justice at the city gate, and who deny the innocent their rights with empty arguments."
Therefore the Lord, who redeemed Abraham, says this about the house of Jacob: "Jacob will no longer be ashamed; his face will no longer grow pale."
"When he sees his children — the work of my hands — among him, they will honor my name. They will honor the Holy One of Jacob and stand in awe of the God of Israel."
"Those who have gone astray in spirit will gain understanding, and those who have complained will accept instruction."

28.text: Now, my brothers and sisters, I have spoken to you as the Spirit has compelled me. I know these things must surely come to pass.
The things written from the book will be of great value to the children of men, and especially to our descendants, who are a remnant of the house of Israel.

In that day, the churches that have been built up — but not for the Lord — will say to one another, "I belong to the Lord!" And the others will say, "I belong to the Lord!" Everyone who has built up churches not for the Lord will say this.
They will argue with one another. Their priests will argue with each other. They will teach with their own learning and deny the Holy Ghost, which gives the ability to speak.

They deny the power of God, the Holy One of Israel. They tell the people, "Listen to us and hear our teaching, because there is no God today. The Lord and Redeemer has finished his work and has given his power to men."
"Listen to our teaching. If someone says a miracle has been performed by the hand of the Lord, do not believe it! He is not a God of miracles today. He has finished his work."
Many will say, "Eat, drink, and be merry, for tomorrow we die, and everything will be fine."

Others will say, "Eat, drink, and be merry. But fear God — he will excuse a little sin. Go ahead, lie a little, take advantage of someone because of what they say, set a trap for your neighbor. There is no harm in this. Do all these things, for tomorrow we die! And if we are guilty, God will only punish us lightly, and in the end we will be saved in the kingdom of God."
Many will teach these false, empty, and foolish doctrines. They will be puffed up in their hearts and try to hide their plans from the Lord. Their works will be done in the dark.
The blood of the saints will cry from the ground against them.
They have all gone off the right path. They have become corrupt.
Because of pride and false teachers and false doctrine, their churches have become corrupt and are puffed up with pride.
They rob the poor because of their fine church buildings. They rob the poor because of their fine clothing. They persecute the humble and poor in heart because of their pride.

They are stiff-necked and carry their heads high. Because of pride, wickedness, abominations, and sexual sin, they have all gone astray except for a few who are the humble followers of Christ. But even these are often led astray because they are taught by the rules of men.

The wise, the educated, the rich who are puffed up with pride, all those who preach false doctrines, all those who commit sexual sin and twist the right way of the Lord — how terrible, terrible, terrible for them, says the Lord God Almighty! They will be thrust down to hell!

How terrible for those who push aside the just for a trivial reason and attack what is good, saying it is worthless! The day will come when the Lord God will swiftly visit the inhabitants of the earth. When they are fully ripe in wickedness, they will be destroyed.
But if the inhabitants of the earth repent of their wickedness and abominations, they will not be destroyed, says the Lord of Hosts.
That great and abominable church — the corruption of all the earth — must fall, and great will be its fall.
The kingdom of the devil must shake. Those who belong to it must be stirred up to repentance, or the devil will grasp them with his everlasting chains. They will be stirred up to anger and perish.
In that day, the devil will rage in the hearts of the children of men and stir them up to anger against what is good.

Others he will pacify and lull into a false sense of security. They will say, 'All is well in Zion! Zion prospers! All is well!' And this is how the devil cheats their souls and leads them carefully down to hell.

Others he flatters and tells them there is no hell. He says to them, 'I am not a devil, because there is no devil.' He whispers in their ears until he grasps them with his awful chains, from which there is no escape.

They are seized by death and hell. Death, hell, the devil, and all who are captured by them must stand before the throne of God and be judged according to their works. Then they must go to the place prepared for them — a lake of fire and brimstone, which is endless torment.
How terrible for the one who is comfortable in Zion!
How terrible for the one who cries, 'All is well!'
How terrible for the one who listens to the teachings of men and denies the power of God and the gift of the Holy Ghost!
How terrible for the one who says, 'We have received God's word, and we need no more!'

In short, how terrible for all who tremble and are angry because of the truth of God! The person who is built on the rock receives it with gladness. The person who is built on a sandy foundation trembles, afraid they will fall.
How terrible for the one who says, 'We have received the word of God, and we need no more of the word of God, for we have enough!'

For the Lord God says: "I will give the children of men line upon line, precept upon precept, here a little and there a little. Blessed are those who listen to my teachings and pay attention to my counsel, for they will learn wisdom. To the one who receives, I will give more. But from those who say, 'We have enough,' I will take away even what they have."
Cursed is the one who puts trust in man or makes flesh their strength, or follows the teachings of men unless those teachings are given by the power of the Holy Ghost.

How terrible for the Gentiles, says the Lord God of Hosts! Even though I reach out my arm to them day after day, they will deny me. Nevertheless, I will be merciful to them, says the Lord God, if they will repent and come to me. For my arm is stretched out all day long, says the Lord God of Hosts.

29.text: But many will say — in that day when I proceed to do a marvelous work among them, to remember my covenants with the children of men, and to reach out my hand a second time to recover my people of the house of Israel —

"I will also remember the promises I made to you, Nephi, and to your father — that I would remember your descendants and that the words of your descendants would come from my mouth to your descendants. My words will go forth to the ends of the earth as a standard for my people of the house of Israel."
"Because my words go forth, many of the Gentiles will say, 'A Bible! A Bible! We have a Bible, and there cannot be any more Bible.'"

But the Lord God says: "O fools! They will have a Bible, and it will come from the Jews, my ancient covenant people. Do they thank the Jews for the Bible they receive from them? Do the Gentiles remember the struggles, labors, and pain of the Jews and their dedication to me in bringing forth salvation to the Gentiles?"

"O you Gentiles, have you remembered the Jews, my ancient covenant people? No! You have cursed them, hated them, and have not tried to help them. But I will return all these things on your own heads, for I the Lord have not forgotten my people."
"You fool who says, 'A Bible, we have a Bible, and we need no more Bible.' Would you even have a Bible if it were not for the Jews?"

"Do you not know that there is more than one nation? Do you not know that I, the Lord your God, have created all people? That I remember those on the islands of the sea? That I rule in the heavens above and on the earth below? And that I bring forth my word to all the nations of the earth?"

"Why do you complain because you will receive more of my word? Do you not know that the testimony of two nations is a witness that I am God, that I remember one nation just like another? I speak the same words to one nation as to another. When the two nations come together, their testimonies will come together too."

"I do this to prove to many that I am the same yesterday, today, and forever, and that I speak my words according to my own will. Just because I have spoken one word does not mean I cannot speak another. My work is not yet finished, and it will not be until the end of mankind — and from that time on, forever."
"Just because you have a Bible, do not think it contains all my words. Do not think I have not had more written."

"I command all people, in the east and the west, the north and the south, and on the islands of the sea, to write down the words I speak to them. From the books that are written, I will judge the world — every person according to their works, according to what is written."

"I will speak to the Jews and they will write it. I will speak to the Nephites and they will write it. I will speak to the other tribes of the house of Israel, whom I have led away, and they will write it. I will speak to all nations of the earth and they will write it."

"The Jews will have the words of the Nephites, and the Nephites will have the words of the Jews. The Nephites and the Jews will have the words of the lost tribes of Israel, and the lost tribes will have the words of the Nephites and the Jews."

"My people of the house of Israel will be gathered home to their lands of possession. My word will also be gathered into one. I will show those who fight against my word and against my people of the house of Israel that I am God, and that I made a covenant with Abraham that I would remember his descendants forever."

30.text: Now, my beloved brothers and sisters, I want to speak to you. I, Nephi, do not want you to think you are more righteous than the Gentiles will be. Unless you keep God's commandments, you will all likewise perish. And because of what has been said, do not think the Gentiles are utterly destroyed.

As many of the Gentiles as will repent are the covenant people of the Lord. And as many of the Jews as will not repent will be cast off. The Lord makes covenants only with those who repent and believe in his Son, who is the Holy One of Israel.

I will prophesy more about the Jews and the Gentiles. After the book I have spoken of comes forth and is written for the Gentiles and sealed up again for the Lord, many will believe the words that are written. They will carry those words to the remnant of our descendants.
Then the remnant of our descendants will know about us — how we came from Jerusalem and that they are descendants of the Jews.
The gospel of Jesus Christ will be declared among them. They will be restored to the knowledge of their ancestors and to the knowledge of Jesus Christ, which their ancestors had.

Then they will rejoice! They will know it is a blessing from the hand of God. The scales of darkness will begin to fall from their eyes. Many generations will not pass before they become a pure and delightful people.
The scattered Jews will also begin to believe in Christ. They will begin to gather on the face of the land. All who believe in Christ will become a delightful people.
The Lord God will begin his work among all nations, peoples, and languages to restore his people on the earth.

With righteousness the Lord God will judge the poor and decide with fairness for the humble of the earth. He will strike the earth with the rod of his mouth, and with the breath of his lips he will slay the wicked.
The time will come quickly when the Lord God will cause a great division among the people. He will destroy the wicked, and he will spare his people — even if he must destroy the wicked by fire.
Righteousness will be his belt, and faithfulness the sash around his waist.
Then the wolf will live with the lamb. The leopard will lie down with the young goat. The calf, the young lion, and the fattened calf will be together, and a little child will lead them.
The cow and the bear will graze together. Their young will lie down side by side. The lion will eat straw like the ox.
An infant will play near the hole of a cobra, and a young child will reach into the viper's den.
They will not hurt or destroy in all my holy mountain, for the earth will be full of the knowledge of the Lord as the waters cover the sea.
The events of all nations will be made known. Everything will be made known to the children of men.
Nothing that is secret will remain hidden. No work of darkness will go unexposed. Nothing sealed on earth will remain locked.

Everything that has been revealed to the children of men will be revealed in that day. Satan will have no power over the hearts of people for a long time. Now, my beloved brothers and sisters, I end my words.

31.text: Now I, Nephi, come to the end of my prophesying. I can only write a few things that I know must come to pass, and I can only write a few of the words of my brother Jacob.
What I have already written is enough, except for a few words I must say about the doctrine of Christ. I will speak to you plainly, according to the plainness of my prophesying.

My soul delights in plainness, because that is how the Lord God works among the children of men. The Lord God gives light to the understanding. He speaks to people according to their own language, so they can understand.
I want you to remember that I told you about the prophet the Lord showed me — the one who would baptize the Lamb of God, who would take away the sins of the world.
If the Lamb of God, being holy, needed to be baptized by water to fulfill all righteousness — then how much more do we, being unholy, need to be baptized by water!
Now I ask you, my beloved brothers and sisters: How did the Lamb of God fulfill all righteousness by being baptized in water?

Don't you know that he was holy? But even though he was holy, he showed the children of men that he humbled himself before the Father in the flesh. He demonstrated to the Father that he would be obedient to him in keeping his commandments.
After he was baptized with water, the Holy Ghost descended upon him in the form of a dove.
This shows the children of men how narrow the path is and how narrow the gate is through which they should enter. He set the example before them.
He said to the children of men: "Follow me." So, my beloved brothers and sisters, can we follow Jesus unless we are willing to keep the commandments of the Father?
The Father said: "Repent, repent, and be baptized in the name of my Beloved Son."

The voice of the Son also came to me, saying: "Whoever is baptized in my name — to him the Father will give the Holy Ghost, just as he gave it to me. Therefore, follow me and do the things you have seen me do."

My beloved brothers and sisters, I know that if you follow the Son with full purpose of heart, with no hypocrisy and no deception before God, but with real intent, repenting of your sins, showing the Father that you are willing to take upon you the name of Christ through baptism — by following your Lord and Savior down into the water according to his word — then you will receive the Holy Ghost. Then comes the baptism of fire and of the Holy Ghost. Then you can speak with the tongue of angels and shout praises to the Holy One of Israel.

But the voice of the Son came to me, saying: "After you have repented of your sins and shown the Father that you are willing to keep my commandments through baptism of water, and have received the baptism of fire and of the Holy Ghost, and can speak with a new tongue — the tongue of angels — if after all this you should deny me, it would have been better for you never to have known me."
I heard a voice from the Father saying: "Yes, the words of my Beloved are true and faithful. He who endures to the end will be saved."
Now I know from this that unless a person endures to the end, following the example of the Son of the living God, they cannot be saved.

Therefore, do the things I told you I have seen that your Lord and Redeemer should do. They were shown to me so you would know the gate by which you should enter. The gate is repentance and baptism by water. Then comes a forgiveness of your sins by fire and by the Holy Ghost.

Then you are on the strait and narrow path that leads to eternal life. You have entered through the gate. You have done according to the commandments of the Father and the Son. You have received the Holy Ghost, which witnesses of the Father and the Son, fulfilling the promise that if you entered in by the way, you would receive.

Now, my beloved brothers and sisters, after you have gotten onto this strait and narrow path, I ask: Is everything done? I say to you, no! You have not come this far except by the word of Christ with unshaken faith in him, relying completely on the merits of him who is mighty to save.

Therefore, you must press forward with steadfastness in Christ, having a perfect brightness of hope and a love of God and of all people. If you press forward, feasting upon the word of Christ, and endure to the end, the Father says: "You will have eternal life."

This is the way. There is no other way or name given under heaven by which a person can be saved in the kingdom of God. This is the doctrine of Christ — the only true doctrine of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost, who is one God, without end. Amen.

32.text: Now I suppose you are wondering in your hearts about what you should do after you have entered the way. But why do you ponder these things in your hearts?
Don't you remember that I told you that after you received the Holy Ghost, you could speak with the tongue of angels? How could you speak with the tongue of angels except by the Holy Ghost?

Angels speak by the power of the Holy Ghost. Therefore, they speak the words of Christ. That is why I said: feast upon the words of Christ, because the words of Christ will tell you everything you should do.
If you cannot understand what I have said, it is because you are not asking and you are not knocking. You are not being brought into the light, and you will perish in the dark.
Again I say: if you will enter by the way and receive the Holy Ghost, it will show you all things that you should do.
This is the doctrine of Christ. No more doctrine will be given until after he shows himself to you in the flesh. When he shows himself to you in the flesh, pay attention to what he says and do it.

Now I, Nephi, cannot say more. The Spirit stops my speech, and I am left to mourn because of the unbelief, wickedness, ignorance, and stubbornness of people. They will not search for knowledge or understand great knowledge when it is given to them in plainness — as plain as words can be.

My beloved brothers and sisters, I can see that you are still pondering in your hearts, and it grieves me that I must speak about this. If you would listen to the Spirit that teaches a person to pray, you would know that you must pray. The evil spirit does not teach a person to pray — it teaches that they should not pray.

You must pray always and never give up. You must not do anything for the Lord without first praying to the Father in the name of Christ, asking him to bless what you do, so that what you do may benefit your soul.

33.text: Now I, Nephi, cannot write all the things that were taught among my people. I am not as powerful in writing as I am in speaking, because when a person speaks by the power of the Holy Ghost, the power of the Holy Ghost carries the message into the hearts of the children of men.
But many people harden their hearts against the Holy Spirit, so it has no place in them. They throw away many of the things that are written and treat them as worthless.

But I, Nephi, have written what I have written, and I consider it of great value, especially to my people. I pray continually for them during the day, and my eyes water my pillow at night because of them. I cry unto my God in faith, and I know he will hear my cry.

I know that the Lord God will bless my prayers for the benefit of my people. The words I have written in weakness will be made strong for them. My writing persuades them to do good, teaches them about their ancestors, speaks of Jesus, and persuades them to believe in him and endure to the end, which is eternal life.
My writing speaks harshly against sin, according to the plainness of the truth. No one will be angry at the words I have written unless they are of the spirit of the devil.
I glory in plainness. I glory in truth. I glory in my Jesus, for he has redeemed my soul from hell.
I have charity for my people and great faith in Christ that I will meet many souls spotless at his judgment seat.
I have charity for the Jews — I say Jews because I mean the people from whom I came.

I also have charity for the Gentiles. But I cannot hope for any of them unless they are reconciled to Christ, enter the narrow gate, walk the strait path that leads to life, and continue on that path until the end of the day of probation.

Now, my beloved brothers and sisters, and also the Jews, and all people everywhere — listen to these words and believe in Christ. If you do not believe in these words, believe in Christ. If you believe in Christ, you will believe in these words, for they are the words of Christ, and he has given them to me. They teach everyone to do good.

If they are not the words of Christ, judge for yourselves — for Christ will show you with power and great glory, at the last day, that they are his words. You and I will stand face to face before his bar, and you will know that I was commanded by him to write these things, despite my weakness.
I pray to the Father in the name of Christ that many of us, if not all, may be saved in his kingdom at that great and last day.
Now, my beloved brothers and sisters, all of the house of Israel, and all people everywhere — I speak to you as the voice of one crying from the dust: Farewell until that great day comes.

And to those who will not partake of the goodness of God and will not respect the words of the Jews, and also my words, and the words that will come from the mouth of the Lamb of God — I bid you an everlasting farewell, for these words will condemn you at the last day.
For what I seal on earth will be brought against you at the judgment bar. The Lord has commanded me, and I must obey. Amen.
